gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور ومعتبر عند ي - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/210)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) إِنِّي سَمِعْتُ مِنْ سَلْمَانَ وَ الْمِقْدَادِ وَ أَبِي ذَرٍّ شَيْئاً مِنْ تَفْسِيرِ الْقُرْآنِ وَ أَحَادِيثَ عَنْ نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) غَيْرَ مَا فِي أَيْدِي النَّاسِ ثُمَّ سَمِعْتُ مِنْكَ تَصْدِيقَ مَا سَمِعْتُ مِنْهُمْ وَ رَأَيْتُ فِي أَيْدِي النَّاسِ أَشْيَاءَ كَثِيرَةً مِنْ تَفْسِيرِ الْقُرْآنِ وَ مِنَ الْأَحَادِيثِ عَنْ نَبِيِّ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَنْتُمْ تُخَالِفُونَهُمْ فِيهَا وَ تَزْعُمُونَ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ كُلَّهُ بَاطِلٌ أَ فَتَرَى النَّاسَ يَكْذِبُونَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مُتَعَمِّدِينَ وَ يُفَسِّرُونَ الْقُرْآنَ بِآرَائِهِمْ

قَالَ فَأَقْبَلَ عَلَيَّ فَقَالَ قَدْ سَأَلْتَ فَافْهَمِ الْجَوَابَ إِنَّ فِي أَيْدِي النَّاسِ حَقّاً وَ بَاطِلًا وَ صِدْقاً وَ كَذِباً وَ نَاسِخاً وَ مَنْسُوخاً وَ عَامّاً وَ خَاصّاً وَ مُحْكَماً وَ مُتَشَابِهاً وَ حِفْظاً وَ وَهَماً وَ قَدْ كُذِبَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) عَلَى عَهْدِهِ حَتَّى قَامَ خَطِيباً فَقَالَ أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ قَدْ كَثُرَتْ عَلَيَّ الْكَذَّابَةُ فَمَنْ كَذَبَ عَلَيَّ مُتَعَمِّداً فَلْيَتَبَوَّأْ مَقْعَدَهُ مِنَ النَّارِ
ثُمَّ كُذِبَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ بَعْدِهِ وَ إِنَّمَا أَتَاكُمُ الْحَدِيثُ مِنْ أَرْبَعَةٍ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ خَامِسٌ

رَجُلٍ مُنَافِقٍ يُظْهِرُ الْإِيمَانَ مُتَصَنِّعٍ بِالْإِسْلَامِ لَا يَتَأَثَّمُ وَ لَا يَتَحَرَّجُ أَنْ يَكْذِبَ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مُتَعَمِّداً فَلَوْ عَلِمَ النَّاسُ أَنَّهُ مُنَافِقٌ كَذَّابٌ لَمْ يَقْبَلُوا مِنْهُ وَ لَمْ يُصَدِّقُوهُ وَ لَكِنَّهُمْ قَالُوا هَذَا قَدْ صَحِبَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ رَآهُ وَ سَمِعَ مِنْهُ وَ أَخَذُوا عَنْهُ وَ هُمْ لَا يَعْرِفُونَ حَالَهُ

وَ قَدْ أَخْبَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَنِ الْمُنَافِقِينَ بِمَا أَخْبَرَهُ وَ وَصَفَهُمْ بِمَا وَصَفَهُمْ فَقَالَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ وَ إِذا رَأَيْتَهُمْ تُعْجِبُكَ أَجْسامُهُمْ وَ إِنْ يَقُولُوا تَسْمَعْ لِقَوْلِهِمْ ثُمَّ بَقُوا بَعْدَهُ فَتَقَرَّبُوا إِلَى أَئِمَّةِ الضَّلَالَةِ وَ الدُّعَاةِ إِلَى النَّارِ بِالزُّورِ وَ الْكَذِبِ وَ الْبُهْتَانِ فَوَلَّوْهُمُ الْأَعْمَالَ وَ حَمَلُوهُمْ عَلَى رِقَابِ النَّاسِ وَ أَكَلُوا بِهِمُ الدُّنْيَا وَ إِنَّمَا النَّاسُ مَعَ الْمُلُوكِ وَ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا مَنْ عَصَمَ اللَّهُ فَهَذَا أَحَدُ الْأَرْبَعَةِ

وَ رَجُلٍ سَمِعَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ شَيْئاً لَمْ يَحْمِلْهُ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ وَ وَهِمَ فِيهِ وَ لَمْ يَتَعَمَّدْ كَذِباً فَهُوَ فِي يَدِهِ يَقُولُ بِهِ وَ يَعْمَلُ بِهِ وَ يَرْوِيهِ فَيَقُولُ أَنَا سَمِعْتُهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَلَوْ عَلِمَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ أَنَّهُ وَهِمَ لَمْ يَقْبَلُوهُ وَ لَوْ عَلِمَ هُوَ أَنَّهُ وَهِمَ لَرَفَضَهُ

وَ رَجُلٍ ثَالِثٍ سَمِعَ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) شَيْئاً أَمَرَ بِهِ ثُمَّ نَهَى عَنْهُ وَ هُوَ لَا يَعْلَمُ أَوْ سَمِعَهُ يَنْهَى عَنْ شَيْ‏ءٍ ثُمَّ أَمَرَ بِهِ وَ هُوَ لَا يَعْلَمُ فَحَفِظَ مَنْسُوخَهُ وَ لَمْ يَحْفَظِ النَّاسِخَ وَ لَوْ عَلِمَ أَنَّهُ مَنْسُوخٌ لَرَفَضَهُ وَ لَوْ عَلِمَ الْمُسْلِمُونَ إِذْ سَمِعُوهُ مِنْهُ أَنَّهُ مَنْسُوخٌ لَرَفَضُوهُ

وَ آخَرَ رَابِعٍ لَمْ يَكْذِبْ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مُبْغِضٍ لِلْكَذِبِ خَوْفاً مِنَ اللَّهِ وَ تَعْظِيماً لِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَمْ يَنْسَهُ بَلْ حَفِظَ مَا سَمِعَ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ فَجَاءَ بِهِ كَمَا سَمِعَ لَمْ يَزِدْ فِيهِ وَ لَمْ يَنْقُصْ مِنْهُ وَ عَلِمَ النَّاسِخَ مِنَ الْمَنْسُوخِ فَعَمِلَ بِالنَّاسِخِ وَ رَفَضَ الْمَنْسُوخَ

فَإِنَّ أَمْرَ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مِثْلُ الْقُرْآنِ نَاسِخٌ وَ مَنْسُوخٌ وَ خَاصٌّ وَ عَامٌّ وَ مُحْكَمٌ وَ مُتَشَابِهٌ قَدْ كَانَ يَكُونُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْكَلَامُ لَهُ وَجْهَانِ كَلَامٌ عَامٌّ وَ كَلَامٌ خَاصٌّ مِثْلُ الْقُرْآنِ وَ قَالَ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فِي كِتَابِهِ ما آتاكُمُ الرَّسُولُ فَخُذُوهُ وَ ما نَهاكُمْ عَنْهُ فَانْتَهُوا

فَيَشْتَبِهُ عَلَى مَنْ لَمْ يَعْرِفْ وَ لَمْ يَدْرِ مَا عَنَى اللَّهُ بِهِ وَ رَسُولُهُ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ لَيْسَ كُلُّ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) كَانَ يَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الشَّيْ‏ءِ فَيَفْهَمُ وَ كَانَ مِنْهُمْ مَنْ يَسْأَلُهُ وَ لَا يَسْتَفْهِمُهُ حَتَّى إِنْ كَانُوا لَيُحِبُّونَ أَنْ يَجِي‏ءَ الْأَعْرَابِيُّ وَ الطَّارِئُ فَيَسْأَلَ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) حَتَّى يَسْمَعُوا

وَ قَدْ كُنْتُ أَدْخُلُ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) كُلَّ يَوْمٍ دَخْلَةً وَ كُلَّ لَيْلَةٍ دَخْلَةً فَيُخَلِّينِي فِيهَا أَدُورُ مَعَهُ حَيْثُ دَارَ وَ قَدْ عَلِمَ أَصْحَابُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَنَّهُ لَمْ يَصْنَعْ ذَلِكَ بِأَحَدٍ مِنَ النَّاسِ غَيْرِي فَرُبَّمَا كَانَ فِي بَيْتِي يَأْتِينِي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَكْثَرُ ذَلِكَ فِي بَيْتِي وَ كُنْتُ إِذَا دَخَلْتُ عَلَيْهِ بَعْضَ مَنَازِلِهِ أَخْلَانِي وَ أَقَامَ عَنِّي نِسَاءَهُ

فَلَا يَبْقَى عِنْدَهُ غَيْرِي وَ إِذَا أَتَانِي لِلْخَلْوَةِ مَعِي فِي مَنْزِلِي لَمْ تَقُمْ عَنِّي فَاطِمَةُ وَ لَا أَحَدٌ مِنْ بَنِيَّ وَ كُنْتُ إِذَا سَأَلْتُهُ أَجَابَنِي وَ إِذَا سَكَتُّ عَنْهُ وَ فَنِيَتْ مَسَائِلِي ابْتَدَأَنِي

فَمَا نَزَلَتْ عَلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) آيَةٌ مِنَ الْقُرْآنِ إِلَّا أَقْرَأَنِيهَا وَ أَمْلَاهَا عَلَيَّ فَكَتَبْتُهَا بِخَطِّي وَ عَلَّمَنِي تَأْوِيلَهَا وَ تَفْسِيرَهَا وَ نَاسِخَهَا وَ مَنْسُوخَهَا وَ مُحْكَمَهَا وَ مُتَشَابِهَهَا وَ خَاصَّهَا وَ عَامَّهَا وَ دَعَا اللَّهَ أَنْ يُعْطِيَنِي فَهْمَهَا وَ حِفْظَهَا فَمَا نَسِيتُ آيَةً مِنْ كِتَابِ اللَّهِ وَ لَا عِلْماً أَمْلَاهُ عَلَيَّ وَ كَتَبْتُهُ مُنْذُ دَعَا اللَّهَ لِي بِمَا دَعَا

وَ مَا تَرَكَ شَيْئاً عَلَّمَهُ اللَّهُ مِنْ حَلَالٍ وَ لَا حَرَامٍ وَ لَا أَمْرٍ وَ لَا نَهْيٍ كَانَ أَوْ يَكُونُ وَ لَا كِتَابٍ مُنْزَلٍ عَلَى أَحَدٍ قَبْلَهُ مِنْ طَاعَةٍ أَوْ مَعْصِيَةٍ إِلَّا عَلَّمَنِيهِ وَ حَفِظْتُهُ فَلَمْ أَنْسَ حَرْفاً وَاحِداً

ثُمَّ وَضَعَ يَدَهُ عَلَى صَدْرِي وَ دَعَا اللَّهَ لِي أَنْ يَمْلَأَ قَلْبِي عِلْماً وَ فَهْماً وَ حُكْماً وَ نُوراً فَقُلْتُ يَا نَبِيَّ اللَّهِ بِأَبِي أَنْتَ وَ أُمِّي مُنْذُ دَعَوْتَ اللَّهَ لِي بِمَا دَعَوْتَ لَمْ أَنْسَ شَيْئاً وَ لَمْ يَفُتْنِي شَيْ‏ءٌ لَمْ أَكْتُبْهُ أَ فَتَتَخَوَّفُ عَلَيَّ النِّسْيَانَ فِيمَا بَعْدُ فَقَالَ لَا لَسْتُ أَتَخَوَّفُ عَلَيْكَ النِّسْيَانَ وَ الْجَهْلَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Ibrahim Bin Umar Al Yamani, from Aban Bin Abu Ayyash, from Suleym Bin Qays Al Hilaly who said,

‘I said to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, ‘I heard from Salman<sup>ra</sup> and Al-Miqdad<sup>ra</sup> and Abu Zarr<sup>ra</sup>, something from the interpretation of the Quran and the Ahadeeth from the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, other than what is in the hands of the people. Then I heard from you<sup>asws</sup> the ratification of what I had heard from them<sup>ra</sup>; and I see a lot of things to be in the hands of the people, from the interpretation of the Quran and from the Ahadeeth from the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. You<sup>asws</sup> are differing from them in it, and you<sup>asws</sup> are claiming that, all of that (which they interpret) is false. So, are you<sup>asws</sup> viewing that the people are lying upon Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> deliberately, and they are interpreting the Quran by their own opinions?’

He (Suleymn Bin Qays) said, ‘So Ali<sup>asws</sup> turned to face me and he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You have asked, so understand the answer. In the hands of the people there is right, and wrong, and truthfulness and lies, and Abrogating and Abrogated, and general and specific, and Decisive and Allegorical, and memorised and delusions. And (it was so) that Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> had been belied upon during his<sup>saww</sup> era, to the extent that he<sup>saww</sup> stood up and addressed, so he<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘O you people! The people have lied a lot upon me<sup>saww</sup>. So the one who lies upon me<sup>saww</sup> deliberately, so let him assume his seat from the Fire’.
Then he<sup>saww</sup> was belied upon from after him<sup>saww</sup>, and rather the Ahadeeth came to you all from four (types of people). There is not fifth (narrator) for them.

A hypocrite man who manifested the Eman pretending with Al-Islam. Neither did he consider it a sin nor anything wrong with deliberately lying upon Rasool-Allahsaww. So, had the people known that he is a lying hypocrite, they would not have accepted from him and would not have believed in him. But, they said, ‘This one has accompanied Rasool-Allahsaww, and had seen himsaww and heard from himsaww’, and they took from him and there were not recognising his state.

And Allahazwj had Informed about the hypocrites with what Heazwj Informed, and Described them with what Heazwj Described them. So Heazwj the Mighty and Majestic Said [63:4] And when you see them, their persons would please you, and If they speak, you will listen to their speech. Then they remained after himsaww, so they went closer to the leaders of misguidance and the inviters of the Fire with the false testimonies, and the lies, and the accusations. Thus, they made them office-bearers and carried them upon ruling over the people, and they (the rulers) devoured the world through them, and rather, the people are (always) with the kings and the world except for the ones whom Allahazwj Protects. So this is one of the four (types of narrators of Ahadeeth)’.

And a man who heard something from Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, but did not preserve it’s all aspect and them became uncertain about it, and he did not lie deliberately. So it was in his hands (and) he was speaking by it, and acting by it, and narrating with it, and he was saying, ‘I heard it from Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>’. So, had the Muslims known that he was deluded, they would not have accepted it, and had he himself known that it was a delusion, he would have rejected it (himself).

And a third man who heard something from Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> he<sup>saww</sup> had ordered with, then he<sup>saww</sup> forbade from it, and he did not know (of the prohibition), or he heard him<sup>saww</sup> forbidding from something, then he<sup>saww</sup> ordered with it, and he did not know (of the order). Thus he preserved the Abrogated and did not preserve the Abrogating, and had he known that he<sup>saww</sup> had Abrogated it, he would have rejected it (himself), and had the Muslims known that when he had heard from him<sup>saww</sup>, it was actually Abrogated, they would have rejected it.

And the fourth (type of narrator) did not lie upon Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, hated the lie out of fear of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and reverence (respect) to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. He did not forget, and he did preserve whatever he heard upon its (correct) aspect. So he came with it just as he had heard, and he did not increase in it and did not reduce from it, and he knew the Abrogating from the Abrogated (orders), thus he acted by the Abrogating and rejected the Abrogated.

So the order of the Prophetsaww is like the (order of the) Quran, Abrogating and Abrogated, and particular and general, and Decisive and Allegorical. There have happened to be the speech from Rasool-Allahsaww having two aspects for it – a general speech and a particular speech, similar to the Quran. And Allahazwj Mighty and Majestic Said in Hisazwj Book [59:7] whatever the Rasool gives you, accept it, and from whatever he forbids you, keep back.

So it was doubtful upon the one who did not recognise and did not know what Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup> Meant by it. And every companions of Rasool-Allah<sup>azwj</sup> was not such that everyone who asked him about something, so he understood; and there were from them ones who were asked and they did not understand, to the extent that they used to love that a Bedouin and a stranger would come over, so he would ask Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> so that they would be listening.

And I<sup>asws</sup> used to go over to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> every day for a meeting, and every night for a meeting. So he<sup>saww</sup> would isolate himself<sup>saww</sup> with me<sup>asws</sup>, going around with him<sup>saww</sup> wherever he<sup>saww</sup> went, and the companions of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> knew that he<sup>saww</sup> did not hold (those meetings) that with anyone from the people apart from me<sup>asws</sup>. So, sometimes it would be in my<sup>asws</sup> house. Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> would mostly hold (consultations) that in my<sup>asws</sup> house; and whenever I<sup>asws</sup> went over to him<sup>saww</sup> in one of his<sup>saww</sup> rooms, he<sup>saww</sup> would isolate with me<sup>asws</sup>, and tell his<sup>saww</sup> womenfolk to arise (to go away) from me<sup>asws</sup>.

So there would not remain in his<sup>saww</sup> presence anyone apart from me<sup>asws</sup>. And whenever he<sup>saww</sup> came over to me<sup>asws</sup> in my<sup>asws</sup> house, Syeda Fatima<sup>asws</sup> would not arise from me<sup>asws</sup>, nor would anyone from my<sup>asws</sup> two sons<sup>asws</sup> (as among the Purified Ones) And it was so that whenever I<sup>asws</sup> asked him<sup>saww</sup>, he<sup>saww</sup> answered me<sup>asws</sup>, and whenever I<sup>asws</sup> was silent from him<sup>saww</sup> and my<sup>asws</sup> questions had finished, he<sup>saww</sup> would initiate (a discussion) me<sup>asws</sup>.

Thus, whatever was Revealed unto Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> a Verse from the Quran except that he<sup>saww</sup> recited it and dictated it to me<sup>asws</sup>. So I<sup>asws</sup> wrote it down by my<sup>asws</sup> own handwriting, and he<sup>saww</sup> taught me<sup>asws</sup> its explanation and its interpretation, and its Abrogating and its Abrogated, and its Decisive and its Allegorical, and its particular and its general. And he<sup>saww</sup> supplicated to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> that He<sup>azwj</sup> should Give me<sup>asws</sup> its understanding and its preservation. So I did not forget a Verse from the Book of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, nor any knowledge dictated unto me<sup>asws</sup>, and I<sup>asws</sup> wrote it down since he<sup>saww</sup> supplicated to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> for me<sup>asws</sup> with what he<sup>saww</sup> supplicated.

And I did not neglect anything which Allah<sup>azwj</sup> had Taught him<sup>saww</sup>, neither from the Permissible nor Prohibition, nor a Command, nor a Forbiddance, occurrences of the past or that of the future, not a Book Revealed unto anyone before him<sup>saww</sup>, from an obedience or a disobedience, except that he<sup>saww</sup> let me<sup>asws</sup> know of it and I<sup>asws</sup> preserved it. Thus, I<sup>asws</sup> did not forget a single letter.

Then hesaww placed hissaww hand upon myasws chest and supplicated to Allahazwj for measws that Heazwj should Fill myasws ‘Qalb’ heart with knowledge, and understanding, and wisdom, and light. So Iasws said: ‘O Prophetsaww of Allahazwj! By myasws fatherasws and myasws motherasws (be for) yousaww! Since yousaww supplicated to Allahazwj for measws with what yousaww supplicated, Iasws never forgot a thing and nothing was missed out by measws that Iasws did not write it, but yousaww are (still) fearing the forgetfulness upon measws?’ So hesaww said: ‘No. It is not out of fearing the forgetfulness and the ignorance upon youasws’’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim ibn Hishim has narrated from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Ibrahim ibn ‘Umar al-Yamani from Aban ibn abu ‘Ayyash from Sulaym ibn Qays al-Hilali who has said the following. “I said to Imam Ali (a.s.), ‘I have heard from Salman, al-Miqdad and abu Dhar certain matters about the interpretations of t he holy Quran and certain Ahadith of the holy Prophet (s.a.) which is different from other have and then have heard from what confirms what I have heard from Salman and others (his friends). I have heard a great deal of Hadith and about the interpretations of the holy Quran. Do you disagree with what have heard from other people and consider such materials as false? Do you think people ascribe lies to the holy Prophet (s.a.) purposely and interpret by their own personal opinions?” The Imam then said, “You have posed a question now listen carefully. “What people have consists of the truth, falsehood, lies, what is abrogating, already abrogated, facts of general nature, of particular natures, clear texts, unclear texts, facts properly memorized and matters that are conjecturally preserved. People had forged certain narration calling them as Hadith of the holy Prophet. The condition was such that once the holy Prophet addressed the people from the pulpit saying, “O people a many lies have spread around and they are considered to be my Hadith. Whoever forges lies and calls them my Hadith has filled up his seat with fire. After the holy Prophet (s.a.) there were more of such lies. There are four kinds of Hadith only and there is no fifth kind. “One narrator of Hadith is a hypocrite who only pretends to be a Muslim. He does consider it a son and offense to lie and calls a narration purposely a Hadith of the holy Prophet while it is not a Hadith of the holy Prophet in reality. If people know that he is hypocrite and a liar they will not believe him. The people say, ‘He lived at the time of the holy Prophet, saw him, has heard Hadith from him and has preserved those Ahadith but they do not know his condition. It is certain that Allah has spoken about the hypocrites the way He has spoken about them and has describe them the way He has describe them. “Their physical appearance attracts you when you see them and when they speak, you carefully listen to them . .” 63:4 They hypocrites then sought closeness to the misguiding leaders and those calling other to hell fire with falsehood, lies and false accusations. The misguiding rulers assigned them for certain offices made them to dominate people. They with their help devoured the world. People follow only the kings and the worldly gains except for those that Allah has protected. This is one of the four kinds. “The other kind is one who has heard Hadith from the holy Prophet by the has preserved it the way it should have been preserved but he is uncertain about it. He has not fabricated anything purposely and has it with him. He speaks about it, practices accordingly and narrates it saying; “ I heard it from the holy Prophet (s.a.). If the Muslims would learn that he is uncertain about it they would accept it from him and if he also would know that there exists uncertainty in it even he also would reject it. “The third kind is one who has heard something form the holy Prophet (s.a.) that contained a command but latter the holy Prophet prohibited it but the man did not have any knowledge of such prohibition. Or that he heard a prohibition form the holy Prophet and then the holy Prophet made it a command but the man did not know of such commandment. In this way preserved the abrogated but not the abrogating but if he knew the abrogating thereof he would have rejected it. If the Muslims knew what they had heard from was abrogated they would also have rejected it. “The last and forth kind is one who has not ascribed a lie to the holy Prophet who hates lies has fear of Allah for respect of the holy Prophet (s.a.). He has not forgotten anything but that he has preserved it the way it should have been preserved. He speaks it just the way has heard with out any addition or omission. He knows which is abrogating and which is abrogated. He thus has practiced according to the abrogating and has rejected the abrogated. There is no doubt that the command so of the holy Prophet (s.a) like the holy Quran is abrogating and abrogated, of general nature and of particular nature, clear text and unclear text. Oftentimes the statements of the holy Prophet may have two aspects: Like a general statement and a statement of a particular nature just like the holy Quran as All, Most Majestic, the Most Glorious, has said, “ . .Take only what the Messenger gives to you and desist from what he forbids you . [59:7]” It may become confusing for one who does not know what exactly Allah and His messenger want people to do. All the companions of the holy Prophet were not as such that would clearly understand the answer to their questions. There were those who would ask a question but would not ask for explanations and would rather love the coming of a Bedouin or a stranger and ask the holy Prophet questions so that he would also listen. “I would go in the presence of the holy Prophet once every day and once every night when he would admit me and we would deal with me the way he wanted. The Sahabah, companions of the Prophet knew that the holy Prophet would not deal with others the he dealt with me. Also the Prophet would come to my house most of the time but when I would go to any one of his houses he would admit me and would ask his wife to leave us alone and then we would have been the only ones therein. When he would come to my house he would ask Fatimah or any of my children to leave the house then whatever would ask he would answer me and when I would have exhausted all of my questions he would begin from his side. Thus, nothing of the holy Quran has ever been revealed the holy prophet had made me to read it and dictated me to and I wrote it down with my own hand writing. He taught me the interpretations of that verse and its explanations, its abrogating or that which was abrogated, the clear texts and the unclear statements, the ones of particular general nature. He would pray to Allah to give me understanding and strong memory. I never forgot any of the verses of the book of Allah or any of the knowledge that he had dictated to me which I wrote them down from the time he prayed to Allah for me. He did not leave any thing of the lawful and unlawful, commands or prohibitions that were there or that would come into being in future or any book that were revealed to anyone before him about the matters of obedience or disobedience that he had not completely taught me and I had not memorized them all. I have not forgotten of them even a single letter. The holy Prophet once placed his hand on my chest and prayed to Allah to fill my heart, with knowledge, proper understanding, wisdom and light. I then said, “O messenger of Allah, may Allah take the soul of my mother and father in service for your cause, from the time you prayed for me I have not forgotten a single matter or missed to write down anything. Do you fear that I might forget them in future? “I do not fear for you any forgetfulness of ignorance.” The holy Prophet (s.a.) replied.’”

translations.bn: আমাদের সাথীদের একটি দল আহমাদ বিন মুহাম্মাদ থেকে, তিনি উসমান বিন ঈসা থেকে, তিনি আবু আইয়ুব আল-খাযযায থেকে, তিনি মুহাম্মাদ বিন মুসলিম থেকে, তিনি আবু আবদিল্লাহ (আলাইহিস সালাম) থেকে বর্ণনা করলেন; তিনি বললেন:
translations.de: Eine Gruppe unserer Gefährten, von Ahmad ibn Muhammad, von Uthman ibn Isa, von Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, von Muhammad ibn Muslim, von Abu Abdillah (Friede sei auf ihm), sagte:
translations.en: A number of our companions, from Ahmad ibn Muhammad, from Uthman ibn Isa, from Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, from Muhammad ibn Muslim, from Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him), who said:
translations.es: Un grupo de nuestros compañeros, de Ahmad ibn Muhammad, de Uthman ibn Isa, de Abu Ayyub al-Jazzaz, de Muhammad ibn Muslim, de Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él), quien dijo:
translations.fa: عده‌ای از اصحاب ما از احمد بن محمد، از عثمان بن عیسی، از ابو ایوب خزاز، از محمد بن مسلم، از ابو عبدالله علیه السلام نقل کردند که گفت:
translations.fr: Un groupe de nos compagnons, d'Ahmad ibn Muhammad, d'Uthman ibn Isa, d'Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, de Muhammad ibn Muslim, d'Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui), dit :
translations.id: Sejumlah sahabat kami, dari Ahmad bin Muhammad, dari Utsman bin Isa, dari Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz, dari Muhammad bin Muslim, dari Abu Abdillah (alaihissalam), berkata:
translations.ru: Группа наших сподвижников, от Ахмада ибн Мухаммада, от Усмана ибн Исы, от Абу Айюба аль-Хаззаза, от Мухаммада ибн Муслима, от Абу Абдиллы (мир ему), который сказал:
translations.tr: Ashabımızdan bir grup, Ahmed b. Muhammed'den, o Osman b. İsa'dan, o Ebu Eyyub el-Hazzaz'dan, o Muhammed b. Müslim'den, o Ebu Abdullah'tan (aleyhisselam) nakletti; dedi ki:
translations.ur: ہمارے اصحاب کی ایک جماعت نے احمد بن محمد سے، انہوں نے عثمان بن عیسیٰ سے، انہوں نے ابو ایوب الخزاز سے، انہوں نے محمد بن مسلم سے، انہوں نے ابو عبداللہ علیہ السلام سے روایت کی، انہوں نے کہا:
translations.zh: 我们的一些同伴，从艾哈迈德·本·穆罕默德，从奥斯曼·本·尔萨，从艾布·艾尤布·哈扎兹，从穆罕默德·本·穆斯里姆，从艾布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）传述，他说：

translations.bn: আমি তাঁকে বললাম: 'এই মানুষদের কি ব্যাপার যারা অমুক-অমুক থেকে, আল্লাহর রাসূলের (সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়া আলিহি) কাছ থেকে বর্ণনা করে, তাদের মিথ্যার ব্যাপারে অভিযুক্ত করা হয় না, তবু আপনাদের কাছ থেকে তার বিপরীত আসে?' তিনি বললেন: 'নিশ্চয়ই হাদিস রহিত হয় যেমন কুরআন রহিত হয়।'
translations.de: Ich sagte zu ihm: 'Was ist die Sache mit den Leuten, die von so-und-so und so-und-so, vom Gesandten Gottes (möge Gottes Segen auf ihm und seiner Familie sein), überliefern — sie werden nicht der Lüge verdächtigt, und dennoch kommt von euch etwas Entgegengesetztes?' Er sagte: 'Wahrlich, der Hadith wird abrogiert, genau so wie der Koran abrogiert wird.'
translations.en: I said to him: 'What is the matter with people who narrate from so-and-so and so-and-so, from the Messenger of God (may God's blessings be upon him and his family), and they are not accused of lying, yet something contrary to it comes from you?' He said: 'Indeed, the hadith is abrogated just as the Quran is abrogated.'
translations.es: Le dije: '¿Cuál es el asunto con las personas que transmiten de fulano y fulano, del Mensajero de Dios (que las bendiciones de Dios sean sobre él y su familia), no son acusados de mentir, sin embargo de vosotros viene algo contrario a ello?' Dijo: 'En verdad, el hadith es abrogado así como el Corán es abrogado.'
translations.fa: به ایشان عرض کردم: «چه وضعی دارند اقوامی که از فلان و فلان، از رسول خدا صلی الله علیه وآله نقل می‌کنند و متهم به دروغ هم نیستند، ولی از شما خلاف آن می‌آید؟» فرمودند: «به درستی که حدیث نسخ می‌شود همان‌طور که قرآن نسخ می‌شود.»
translations.fr: Je lui dis : 'Quelle est l'affaire des gens qui transmettent de tel et tel, du Messager de Dieu (que les bénédictions de Dieu soient sur lui et sa famille), on ne les accuse pas de mentir, et pourtant de vous vient son contraire ?' Il dit : 'En vérité, le hadith est abrogé de même que le Coran est abrogé.'
translations.id: Aku berkata kepadanya: 'Apa masalahnya dengan orang-orang yang meriwayatkan dari si fulan dan si fulan, dari Rasulullah (shallallahu alaihi wa alihi), mereka tidak dituduh berdusta, namun dari kalian datang sesuatu yang bertentangan dengannya?' Beliau berkata: 'Sesungguhnya hadits dapat dinasakh sebagaimana Al-Qur'an dinasakh.'
translations.ru: Я сказал ему: «В чём дело с людьми, которые передают от такого-то и такого-то, от Посланника Аллаха (да благословит его Аллах и его семью), их не обвиняют во лжи, однако от вас приходит противоположное?» Он сказал: «Поистине, хадис отменяется, подобно тому как отменяется Коран.»
translations.tr: Ona dedim ki: 'Falan ve filandan, Allah'ın Resûlü'nden aktaran bu insanların durumu nedir? Onlar yalancılıkla itham edilmiyorlar, ama sizden bunun aksine bir şey geliyor.' O dedi ki: 'Gerçekten hadis, tıpkı Kur'an'ın neshedildiği gibi neshedilir.'
translations.ur: میں نے ان سے کہا: 'یہ لوگ فلاں اور فلاں سے، جو رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم سے روایت کرتے ہیں، ان پر جھوٹ کا الزام نہیں، پھر آپ کی طرف سے اس کے خلاف کیوں آتا ہے؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'بے شک حدیث منسوخ ہوتی ہے جیسے قرآن منسوخ ہوتا ہے۔'
translations.zh: 我对他说：'那些从某某人、某某人传述安拉使者（愿主赐福于他及其家属）言论的人是怎么回事？他们不被指控说谎，然而你们那里却传来与之相反的内容。'他说：'圣训可被废止，正如《古兰经》可被废止一样。'

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عُثْمَانَ بْنِ عِيسَى عَنْ أَبِي أَيُّوبَ الْخَزَّازِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا بَالُ أَقْوَامٍ يَرْوُونَ عَنْ فُلَانٍ وَ فُلَانٍ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ لَا يُتَّهَمُونَ بِالْكَذِبِ فَيَجِيءُ مِنْكُمْ خِلَافُهُ قَالَ إِنَّ الْحَدِيثَ يُنْسَخُ كَمَا يُنْسَخُ الْقُرْآنُ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Collective anonymous designation for al-Kulayni's direct teachers; specific individuals not named
Likely Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid al-Barqi or Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Isa al-Ash'ari, both prominent narrators in this chain
narrators.known_identity: Ahmad ibn Muhammad (al-Barqi or al-Ash'ari)
Uthman ibn Isa al-Rawwasi al-Kilabi, a companion of Imam al-Sadiq and al-Kazim
Abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz Ibrahim ibn Uthman, a companion of Imam al-Sadiq
Muhammad ibn Muslim ibn Riyah al-Thaqafi, one of the most prominent companions of Imams al-Baqir and al-Sadiq, known for transmitting 30,000 hadiths from al-Baqir
Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad al-Sadiq (AS), 6th Imam, d. 148 AH

The Quran is abrogated

bn.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (রাসূলুল্লাহ): আল্লাহর রাসূল — নবী মুহাম্মাদ (সাল্লাল্লাহু আলাইহি ওয়া আলিহি)
bn.فُلَان (ফুলান): অমুক — পরিচয় না দিয়ে কাউকে বোঝানোর জন্য আরবিতে ব্যবহৃত শব্দ
bn.نَسْخ (নসখ): রহিতকরণ — ইসলামি আইনি নীতি যেখানে পরবর্তী বিধান পূর্ববর্তীটিকে বাতিল করে

de.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (Rasul Allah): Gesandter Gottes — bezieht sich auf den Propheten Muhammad (SAWW)
de.فُلَان (Fulan): So-und-so — arabische anonyme Bezeichnung für nicht genannte Personen
de.نَسْخ (Naskh): Abrogation — islamisch-rechtliches Prinzip, durch das eine spätere Vorschrift eine frühere aufhebt

en.خِلَاف: Contradiction/opposition — the narrator observes that the Imam's teachings sometimes contradict reports attributed to the Prophet.
en.رَسُول اللَّه: The Messenger of God — referring to the Prophet Muhammad (SAWW).
en.فُلَان: So-and-so — an anonymous placeholder in Arabic used to refer to unnamed persons without specifying identity.
en.نَسْخ: Abrogation — the Islamic legal principle whereby a later ruling supersedes an earlier one. Applied here to hadith, not just Quran.

es.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (Rasul Allah): El Mensajero de Dios — refiriéndose al Profeta Muhammad (SAWW)
es.فُلَان (fulano): Fulano — término árabe anónimo para referirse a personas no nombradas
es.نَسْخ (naskh): Abrogación — principio jurídico islámico por el cual una disposición posterior deroga una anterior

fa.رسول الله: فرستاده خدا — پیامبر اکرم صلی الله علیه وآله
fa.فلان: فلان — اشاره به افراد ناشناس در عربی
fa.نسخ: نسخ — اصل اسلامی که حکم متأخر حکم متقدم را ملغی می‌سازد

fr.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (Rasûl Allah): Le Messager de Dieu — désignant le Prophète Muhammad (SAWW)
fr.فُلَان (fulan): Untel — terme arabe anonyme désignant des personnes non nommées
fr.نَسْخ (naskh): Abrogation — principe juridique islamique par lequel une disposition postérieure annule une disposition antérieure

id.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ (Rasulullah): Utusan Allah — merujuk pada Nabi Muhammad (SAWW)
id.فُلَان (fulan): Si fulan — pengganti anonim dalam bahasa Arab untuk menyebut seseorang yang tidak disebutkan namanya
id.نَسْخ (nasakh): Penghapusan/pembatalan — prinsip hukum Islam di mana hukum yang lebih baru menghapus yang lebih lama

ru.فُلَان (фулан): Такой-то — арабское анонимное обозначение для неназванных лиц
ru.نَسْخ (насх): Отмена/аброгация — принцип исламского права, согласно которому более поздние нормы отменяют более ранние

tr.فُلَان (falan): Falan — kimliği belirtilmeyen kişiler için Arapça yer tutucu ifade
tr.نَسْخ (nesih): Abrogasyon — sonraki bir hükmün öncekini geçersiz kılması ilkesi

ur.رسول اللہ: اللہ کے رسول — نبی محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم
ur.فلاں: فلاں — ایک گمنام شخص کے لیے عربی اشارہ
ur.نسخ: منسوخی — اسلامی اصول جس میں بعد کا حکم پہلے کو ختم کر دیتا ہے

zh.رَسُولُ اللَّهِ（真主的使者）: 安拉的使者——指先知穆罕默德（主赐福于他及其家属）
zh.فُلَان（某某人）: 阿拉伯语匿名占位词，指不具名的人
zh.نَسْخ（纳斯赫）: 废止——伊斯兰法律原则，即较晚的规定废止较早的规定

seo_questions.bn: ইমামের শিক্ষা কেন মাঝে মাঝে নবীর হাদিসের সাথে ভিন্ন?
seo_questions.de: Warum weichen die Lehren des Imams manchmal von den dem Propheten zugeschriebenen Hadithen ab?
seo_questions.en: Why do the Imam's teachings sometimes differ from hadiths attributed to the Prophet?
seo_questions.es: ¿Por qué las enseñanzas del Imam a veces difieren de los hadices atribuidos al Profeta?
seo_questions.fa: چرا گاهی تعالیم امام با احادیث منسوب به پیامبر تفاوت دارد؟
seo_questions.fr: Pourquoi les enseignements de l'Imam diffèrent-ils parfois des hadiths attribués au Prophète ?
seo_questions.id: Mengapa ajaran Imam terkadang berbeda dari hadits yang dikaitkan dengan Nabi?
seo_questions.ru: Почему учение Имама иногда расходится с хадисами, приписываемыми Пророку?
seo_questions.tr: İmam'ın öğretileri neden zaman zaman Peygamber'e atfedilen hadislerle çelişir?
seo_questions.ur: امام کی تعلیمات بعض اوقات نبی سے منسوب احادیث سے مختلف کیوں ہوتی ہیں؟
seo_questions.zh: 为什么伊玛目的教导有时与归于先知的圣训不同？

similar_content_hints.description: A hadith explaining that when two narrations contradict each other, the one that agrees with the Quran is preferred and the one that contradicts it is rejected, providing another framework for resolving hadith contradictions.

Narrations from the Imams explaining that they sometimes give different rulings to different questioners according to the circumstances (taqiyya or differing contexts), which provides another reason for apparent contradictions in their teachings.
A hadith about the principle of taking the later of two contradictory narrations, reflecting a similar hermeneutical principle applied to abrogation within the Prophetic traditions.

summaries.bn: ইমামদের সবচেয়ে প্রভাবশালী সাথীদের একজন মুহাম্মাদ বিন মুসলিম ইমাম আল-সাদিকের কাছে একটি আপাত-বিরোধিতার প্রশ্ন উপস্থাপন করেন: নির্ভরযোগ্য বর্ণনাকারীরা নবী থেকে হাদিস নকল করেন, কিন্তু ইমামের শিক্ষা মাঝে মাঝে ভিন্ন। ইমাম নাসখ (রহিতকরণ) নীতির মাধ্যমে উত্তর দেন: যেমন কুরআনের পরবর্তী আয়াত আগেরটিকে রহিত করতে পারে, তেমনি পরবর্তী হাদিস আগেরটিকে রহিত করতে পারে।
summaries.de: Muhammad ibn Muslim, einer der herausragendsten Gefährten der Imame, stellt Imam al-Sadiq eine Frage über einen scheinbaren Widerspruch: Zuverlässige Überlieferer berichten Hadithe vom Propheten, doch die Lehren des Imams weichen manchmal davon ab. Der Imam antwortet mit dem Prinzip der Abrogation (Naskh): Ebenso wie spätere Koranverse frühere aufheben können, können spätere Hadithe frühere abrogieren. Diese Lehre war grundlegend für das schiitische hermeneutische Verständnis widersprüchlicher Überlieferungen.
summaries.en: Muhammad ibn Muslim, one of the most prolific companions of the Imams, poses a question to Imam al-Sadiq about an apparent contradiction: trustworthy narrators transmit hadiths from the Prophet, yet the Imam's teachings sometimes differ. The Imam resolves this by invoking the principle of abrogation (naskh) — just as later Quranic verses can supersede earlier ones, later prophetic hadiths can abrogate earlier ones. This teaching was essential for understanding the Shia hermeneutical approach to contradictory narrations, where the Imam's knowledge of which hadith abrogated which gives him an authoritative role in resolving such conflicts.
summaries.es: Muhammad ibn Muslim, uno de los más destacados compañeros de los Imames, plantea al Imam al-Sadiq una aparente contradicción: transmisores confiables relatan hadices del Profeta, pero las enseñanzas del Imam a veces difieren. El Imam responde invocando el principio de abrogación (naskh): así como los versículos posteriores del Corán pueden derogar los anteriores, los hadices posteriores pueden abrogar los anteriores. Esta enseñanza era esencial para comprender el enfoque hermenéutico shií ante las narraciones contradictorias.
summaries.fa: محمد بن مسلم، از برجسته‌ترین اصحاب ائمه، از امام صادق علیه السلام درباره تناقضی ظاهری سؤال می‌کند: راویان مورد اعتماد از پیامبر احادیثی نقل می‌کنند که با تعالیم امام مغایرت دارد. امام با استناد به اصل نسخ پاسخ می‌دهند: همان‌طور که آیات قرآن می‌توانند یکدیگر را نسخ کنند، احادیث متأخر نیز می‌توانند احادیث متقدم را نسخ کنند. این آموزش برای فهم رویکرد هرمنوتیکی شیعه در تعارض روایات اهمیت بنیادی دارد.
summaries.fr: Muhammad ibn Muslim, l'un des plus éminents compagnons des Imams, pose à l'Imam al-Sadiq une question sur une apparente contradiction : des transmetteurs fiables rapportent des hadiths du Prophète, mais les enseignements de l'Imam diffèrent parfois. L'Imam répond en invoquant le principe d'abrogation (naskh) : de même que des versets coraniques ultérieurs peuvent abroger des versets antérieurs, des hadiths ultérieurs peuvent en abroger d'autres. Cet enseignement était fondamental pour comprendre l'approche herméneutique chiite face aux narrations contradictoires.
summaries.id: Muhammad bin Muslim, salah satu sahabat paling terkemuka para Imam, mengajukan pertanyaan kepada Imam al-Shadiq tentang kontradiksi yang tampak: perawi terpercaya menyampaikan hadits dari Nabi, namun ajaran Imam terkadang berbeda. Imam menjawab dengan prinsip nasakh (pembatalan): sebagaimana ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an dapat menghapus ayat sebelumnya, hadits yang datang kemudian dapat menasakh hadits yang lebih awal. Ajaran ini sangat penting bagi pemahaman hermeneutika Syiah dalam menyelesaikan pertentangan antar riwayat.
summaries.ru: Мухаммад ибн Муслим, один из наиболее выдающихся сподвижников Имамов, задаёт Имаму ас-Садику вопрос о видимом противоречии: надёжные передатчики приводят хадисы от Пророка, однако учение Имама иногда расходится с ними. Имам отвечает, апеллируя к принципу отмены (насх): подобно тому как более поздние аяты Корана могут отменять более ранние, более поздние хадисы могут отменять более ранние. Это учение было основополагающим для понимания шиитского герменевтического подхода к противоречивым преданиям.
summaries.tr: İmamların en önde gelen ashabından biri olan Muhammed b. Müslim, İmam Sadık'a görünürdeki bir çelişkiyi soruyor: Güvenilir ravilerin Peygamber'den naklettiği hadislerle İmam'ın öğretileri bazen çelişmektedir. İmam, nesih ilkesiyle cevap veriyor: Kur'an'da sonraki ayetlerin öncekini nesh edebildiği gibi, sonraki hadisler de öncekini nesh edebilir. Bu öğreti, çelişkili rivayetleri anlamlandırmada Şii yorumsal yaklaşımı için temel önem taşır.
summaries.ur: محمد بن مسلم، جو اماموں کے سب سے مشہور اصحاب میں سے ہیں، امام صادق علیہ السلام سے ایک بظاہر تضاد کے بارے میں سوال پوچھتے ہیں: ثقہ راوی پیغمبر سے احادیث نقل کرتے ہیں، مگر امام کی تعلیمات کبھی کبھی مختلف ہوتی ہیں۔ امام نے نسخ کے اصول سے جواب دیا کہ جیسے قرآن میں بعد کی آیت پہلے کو منسوخ کر سکتی ہے، ویسے ہی بعد کی حدیث پہلے کو منسوخ کر دیتی ہے۔ یہ تعلیم متضاد روایات کو سمجھنے کے شیعہ طریقہ میں بنیادی اہمیت رکھتی ہے۔
summaries.zh: 穆罕默德·本·穆斯里姆是伊玛目最杰出的伴侣之一，他向伊玛目萨迪克提出了一个表面上的矛盾：可信的传述者从先知那里传述圣训，但伊玛目的教导有时与之不同。伊玛目援引废止（纳斯赫）原则作答：正如《古兰经》中较晚的经文可以废止较早的，较晚的圣训也可以废止较早的。这一教导对于理解什叶派在对待矛盾传述时的诠释学方法至关重要。

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/215)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا بَالُ أَقْوَامٍ يَرْوُونَ عَنْ فُلَانٍ وَ فُلَانٍ عَنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَا يُتَّهَمُونَ بِالْكَذِبِ فَيَجِي‏ءُ مِنْكُمْ خِلَافُهُ قَالَ إِنَّ الْحَدِيثَ يُنْسَخُ كَمَا يُنْسَخُ الْقُرْآنُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from usman Bin Isa, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazzaz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is the matter that the people are reporting from so and so, from Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, you<sup>asws</sup> are not accusing them of the lying, and there is coming from you<sup>asws</sup>, different to it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The Hadeeth Abrogates just as the Quran Abrogates’.
en.sarwar: 2. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ‘Uthman ibn ’Isa from abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following. “I explained to Imam abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) saying ‘What is the matter with people who narrate from so and so from the holy Prophet (s.a.) whom they do not accuse of telling lies and thing and from you facts against it comes to light? The Imam (a.s.) said, “Hadith becomes abrogated just as it happens with the holy Quran.’”

translations.bn: আমি আবু আবদিল্লাহ (আলাইহিস সালাম)-কে বললাম: 'আমার কী হলো যে আমি আপনাকে একটি প্রশ্ন করি আর আপনি আমাকে একটি উত্তর দেন, তারপর আমি ছাড়া অন্য কেউ আপনার কাছে আসে এবং আপনি তাকে একই প্রশ্নে ভিন্ন উত্তর দেন?' তিনি বললেন: 'আমরা মানুষকে বৃদ্ধি ও হ্রাস (তাদের সক্ষমতা) অনুযায়ী উত্তর দিই।'
translations.de: Ich sagte zu Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm): «Was ist mit mir, dass ich dich eine Frage stelle und du mir eine Antwort gibst, dann kommt jemand anderes und du antwortest ihm anders auf dieselbe Frage?» Er sagte: «Wir antworten den Menschen gemäß Mehrung und Minderung (ihrer Fähigkeit).»
translations.en: I said to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): 'What is the matter with me that I ask you a question and you answer me with one answer, then someone other than me comes to you, and you answer him with a different answer about the same question?' So he said: 'We answer people according to increase and decrease (of their capacity).'
translations.es: Dije a Abu Abdillah (paz sea con él): '¿Cuál es el asunto conmigo que le pregunto sobre una cuestión y me responde con una respuesta, luego viene a usted alguien distinto a mí y le responde con una respuesta diferente?' Dijo: 'Respondemos a la gente según el aumento y la disminución (de su capacidad).'
translations.fa: به ابوعبدالله علیه السلام گفتم: «چه شده است که من از شما مسأله‌ای می‌پرسم و شما جوابی می‌دهید، سپس غیر من پیشتان می‌آید و شما همان مسأله را با جوابی دیگر پاسخ می‌دهید؟» فرمود: «ما مردم را بر اساس زیادی و کمی (ظرفیتشان) پاسخ می‌دهیم.»
translations.fr: J'ai dit à Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui) : « Qu'est-ce qui me prend que je te pose une question et tu me réponds avec une réponse, puis quelqu'un d'autre que moi vient à toi et tu lui réponds avec une autre réponse à la même question ? » Il dit : « Nous répondons aux gens selon le plus et le moins (de leur capacité). »
translations.id: Aku berkata kepada Abu Abdillah (alaihis salam): 'Apa yang terjadi padaku bahwa aku bertanya kepadamu tentang suatu masalah lalu kamu menjawabku dengan satu jawaban, kemudian datang orang lain selain aku kepadamu lalu kamu menjawabnya dengan jawaban yang berbeda?' Maka beliau bersabda: 'Kami menjawab manusia sesuai dengan penambahan dan pengurangan (kapasitas mereka).'
translations.ru: Я сказал Абу Абдиллаху (мир ему): «Что происходит со мной — я задаю тебе вопрос, и ты отвечаешь мне одним ответом, затем приходит кто-то другой, и ты отвечаешь ему иначе?» Он сказал: «Мы отвечаем людям сообразно умножению и убыли (их способностей).»
translations.tr: Ebu Abdullah'a (a.s.) dedim: 'Benim halim nedir ki sana bir mesele soruyor ve bana bir cevap veriyorsun, sonra benden başkası geliyor ve ona aynı meselede farklı bir cevap veriyorsun?' Buyurdu: 'Biz insanlara artırma ve azaltmaya (kapasitelerine) göre cevap veririz.'
translations.ur: میں نے ابو عبد اللہ علیہ السلام سے عرض کیا: 'میرا کیا حال ہے کہ میں آپ سے کوئی مسئلہ پوچھتا ہوں تو آپ مجھے ایک جواب دیتے ہیں، پھر میرے علاوہ کوئی اور آپ کے پاس آتا ہے تو آپ اسے اسی مسئلے میں دوسرا جواب دیتے ہیں؟' تو آپ نے فرمایا: 'ہم لوگوں کو زیادتی اور نقصان (ان کی استعداد) کے مطابق جواب دیتے ہیں۔'
translations.zh: 我对阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）说：'我怎么了，我向您问一个问题，您给我一个答案，然后除我之外的人来，您却就同一问题给出不同答案？'他说：'我们根据（他们能力的）增减回答众人。'

translations.bn: সে বলল: আমি বললাম: 'তাহলে আমাকে আল্লাহর রাসূলের সাহাবীদের সম্পর্কে জানান — তারা কি সত্যবাদিতার সাথে বর্ণনা করেছেন নাকি মিথ্যা বলেছেন?' তিনি বললেন: 'বরং তারা সত্যবাদী ছিলেন।' আমি বললাম: 'তাহলে তাদের মধ্যে মতভেদ কেন?' তিনি বললেন: 'তুমি কি জানো না যে একজন লোক রাসূলের কাছে আসত, প্রশ্ন করত, উত্তর পেত, তারপর এমন উত্তর পেত যা প্রথম উত্তরকে রহিত করে দিত? ফলে হাদিসগুলো একে অপরকে রহিত করে দিয়েছে।'
translations.de: Er sagte: Ich sagte: «Informiere mich über die Gefährten des Gesandten Gottes — sprachen sie die Wahrheit oder logen sie?» Er sagte: «Sie sprachen die Wahrheit.» Ich sagte: «Warum stimmten sie nicht überein?» Er sagte: «Weißt du nicht, dass ein Mann zum Gesandten Gottes kam, fragte und eine Antwort erhielt, dann eine weitere erhielt, die die erste aufhob? So hoben die Ahadith einander auf.»
translations.en: He said: I said: 'Then inform me about the companions of the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) — did they narrate truthfully from Muhammad (peace be upon him and his family) or did they lie?' He said: 'Rather, they were truthful.' I said: 'Then what is the matter with them that they differed?' So he said: 'Do you not know that a man would come to the Messenger of God (peace be upon him and his family) and ask him a question, and he would answer him regarding it with an answer, then he would answer him after that with what abrogated that answer — and so the narrations abrogated one another?'
translations.es: Dijo: Dije: '¿Entonces infórmame sobre los compañeros del Mensajero de Dios — fueron veraces o mintieron?' Dijo: 'Más bien, fueron veraces.' Dije: '¿Por qué discreparon?' Dijo: '¿Acaso no sabes que un hombre solía venir al Mensajero de Dios, preguntarle y recibir una respuesta, luego recibir otra que abrogaba la primera? Así los ahadith se abrogaron unos a otros.'
translations.fa: گفت: عرض کردم: «پس مرا از اصحاب رسول الله صلی الله علیه وآله خبر دهید — آیا صادق بودند یا دروغ گفتند؟» فرمود: «بلکه صادق بودند.» گفتم: «پس چرا اختلاف داشتند؟» فرمود: «آیا نمی‌دانی که مردی پیش رسول الله می‌آمد، سؤال می‌کرد، جواب می‌گرفت، سپس جوابی می‌گرفت که آن را نسخ می‌کرد؟ پس احادیث یکدیگر را نسخ کردند.»
translations.fr: Il dit : Je dis : « Alors informe-moi au sujet des compagnons du Messager de Dieu (que la paix et les bénédictions de Dieu soient sur lui et sa famille) — ont-ils dit la vérité au sujet de Muhammad (que la paix soit sur lui et sa famille) ou ont-ils menti ? » Il dit : « Ils ont dit la vérité. » Je dis : « Alors pourquoi ont-ils divergé ? » Il dit : « Ne sais-tu pas qu'un homme avait coutume de venir au Messager de Dieu (que la paix soit sur lui et sa famille), de lui poser une question, et il lui répondait avec une réponse, puis après cela il lui répondait avec ce qui abrogeait cette réponse ? Ainsi les ahadith s'abrogèrent les uns les autres. »
translations.id: Ia berkata: Aku berkata: 'Maka beritahukan aku tentang para sahabat Rasulullah (shallallahu alaihi wa alihi) — apakah mereka jujur atau berdusta?' Beliau bersabda: 'Bahkan mereka jujur.' Aku berkata: 'Lalu apa yang membuat mereka berselisih?' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidakkah kamu tahu bahwa seseorang biasa datang kepada Rasulullah dan bertanya, lalu beliau menjawab, kemudian menjawab setelah itu dengan apa yang menasakh jawaban itu? Maka hadis-hadis saling menasakh satu sama lain.'
translations.ru: Он сказал: Я сказал: «Сообщи мне о сподвижниках Посланника Бога — были ли они правдивы или лгали?» Он сказал: «Напротив, они были правдивы.» Я сказал: «Тогда почему они расходились?» Он сказал: «Разве ты не знаешь, что человек приходил к Посланнику Бога, спрашивал и получал ответ, затем получал другой, отменявший первый? Так хадисы отменяли друг друга.»
translations.tr: Dedi: Dedim: 'O halde bana Allah'ın Resulü'nün ashabı hakkında bilgi ver — doğru mu söylediler yoksa yalan mı?' Buyurdu: 'Aksine, doğru söylediler.' Dedim: 'Neden ihtilaf ettiler?' Buyurdu: 'Bilmiyor musun ki adam Resulullah'a gelir sorardı, cevap alırdı, sonra onu nesheden cevap alırdı? Böylece hadisler birbirini neshedip gitti.'
translations.ur: اس نے کہا: میں نے کہا: 'تو مجھے رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ کے اصحاب کے بارے میں بتائیں — کیا انہوں نے محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ سے سچ بیان کیا یا جھوٹ؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'بلکہ انہوں نے سچ کہا۔' میں نے کہا: 'تو ان میں اختلاف کیوں ہوا؟' آپ نے فرمایا: 'کیا تم نہیں جانتے کہ آدمی رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ کے پاس آتا تھا، مسئلہ پوچھتا تھا، آپ جواب دیتے تھے، پھر بعد میں ایسا جواب دیتے جو پہلے جواب کو منسوخ کر دیتا تھا؟ اس طرح احادیث نے ایک دوسرے کو منسوخ کر دیا۔'
translations.zh: 他说：我说：'那么请告诉我关于真主使者的同伴——他们是如实传述还是撒谎？'他说：'不，他们是真实的。'我说：'那他们为何存在分歧？'他说：'你难道不知道，有人来到真主使者面前，请教问题，得到答案，之后又得到一个废止该答案的答案？因此这些圣训相互废止了对方。'

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي نَجْرَانَ عَنْ عَاصِمِ بْنِ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ مَا بَالِي أَسْأَلُكَ عَنِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَتُجِيبُنِي فِيهَا بِالْجَوَابِ ثُمَّ يَجِيئُكَ غَيْرِي فَتُجِيبُهُ فِيهَا بِجَوَابٍ آخَرَ فَقَالَ إِنَّا نُجِيبُ النَّاسَ عَلَى الزِّيَادَةِ وَالنُّقْصَانِ قَالَ قُلْتُ فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ صَدَقُوا عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ أَمْ كَذَبُوا قَالَ بَلْ صَدَقُوا قَالَ قُلْتُ فَمَا بَالُهُمُ اخْتَلَفُوا فَقَالَ أَمَا تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ الرَّجُلَ كَانَ يَأْتِي رَسُولَ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَآلِهِ فَيَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَيُجِيبُهُ فِيهَا بِالْجَوَابِ ثُمَّ يُجِيبُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مَا يَنْسَخُ ذَلِكَ الْجَوَابَ فَنَسَخَتِ الْأَحَادِيثُ بَعْضُهَا بَعْضاً

bn.الزيادة والنقصان: বৃদ্ধি ও হ্রাস — বিভিন্ন প্রশ্নকারীর আধ্যাত্মিক সক্ষমতা ও বোঝার স্তরের ইঙ্গিত
bn.نسخ: নাসখ (রহিতকরণ) — ইসলামী আইনে পরবর্তী বিধান দ্বারা পূর্ববর্তী বিধান রহিত হওয়ার নীতি

de.الزيادة والنقصان: Mehrung und Minderung — Verweis auf die unterschiedliche geistige Kapazität und das Verständnisniveau verschiedener Fragesteller
de.نسخ: Abrogation (Naskh) — islamisches Rechtsprinzip, wonach eine spätere Bestimmung eine frühere aufhebt

en.اختلفوا: They differed — referring to apparent contradictions among the Prophet's companions in their narrations
en.الزيادة والنقصان: Increase and decrease — referring to the varying spiritual capacity, readiness, and level of understanding of different questioners
en.نسخ/نسخت: Abrogation — the Islamic legal and Quranic doctrine whereby a later ruling supersedes an earlier one; here applied to hadith narrations

es.الزيادة والنقصان: Aumento y disminución — referido a la capacidad espiritual y nivel de comprensión variable de cada preguntante
es.نسخ: Abrogación (naskh) — el principio islámico por el cual una disposición posterior deroga una anterior

fa.الزيادة والنقصان: زیادی و کمی — اشاره به ظرفیت روحانی و سطح فهم مختلف سائلان
fa.نسخ: نسخ — اصلی در فقه اسلامی که حکم متأخر حکم متقدم را ساقط می‌کند

fr.الزيادة والنقصان: Augmentation et diminution — référence à la capacité spirituelle variable et au niveau de compréhension de chaque questionneur
fr.نسخ: Abrogation (naskh) — principe islamique par lequel une disposition postérieure abroge une antérieure

id.الزيادة والنقصان: Penambahan dan pengurangan — merujuk pada kapasitas spiritual dan tingkat pemahaman berbeda dari para penanya
id.نسخ: Nasakh — doktrin hukum Islam di mana ketentuan yang datang kemudian menghapus yang sebelumnya

ru.الزيادة والنقصان: Умножение и убыль — ссылка на различную духовную способность и уровень понимания разных спрашивающих
ru.نسخ: Отмена (насх) — исламский правовой принцип, по которому позднее предписание отменяет более раннее

tr.الزيادة والنقصان: Artırma ve azaltma — farklı soranların ruhani kapasitesine ve anlayış düzeyine atıfta bulunur
tr.نسخ: Nesih — İslam hukuku ve Kur'an'da sonraki bir hükmün öncekinin yerini alması

ur.الزيادة والنقصان: زیادتی اور نقصان — مختلف سائلین کی روحانی صلاحیت اور سمجھ کی سطح کا اشارہ
ur.نسخ: نسخ — بعد کے حکم کا پہلے کے حکم کو ختم کرنا

zh.الزيادة والنقصان: 增减——指不同提问者在精神能力和理解水平上的差异
zh.نسخ: 废止——伊斯兰法律和《古兰经》中后来的律令取代先前律令的原则

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম সাদিক কেন বিভিন্ন লোককে একই প্রশ্নের ভিন্ন উত্তর দিতেন?

seo_questions.en: Why did Imam al-Sadiq give different answers to different people asking the same question?
seo_questions.es: ¿Por qué el Imam al-Sadiq daba respuestas diferentes a distintas personas ante la misma pregunta?
seo_questions.fa: چرا امام صادق به سائلان مختلف جواب‌های متفاوت می‌داد؟
seo_questions.fr: Pourquoi l'Imam al-Sadiq donnait-il des réponses différentes à des personnes différentes posant la même question ?

similar_content_hints.description: Other hadiths from al-Kafi's chapter on hadith differences where Imam al-Sadiq explains why apparent contradictions in hadith can arise, including the principle of taqiyya (precautionary dissimulation) as another reason for variant answers.

Hadiths discussing the principle that the Imams give different rulings according to the questioner's level of understanding and spiritual readiness (al-ziyadah wa'l-nuqsan), related to esoteric vs exoteric knowledge.
Hadiths on the doctrine of abrogation (naskh) in the Quran and Sunnah, including discussions of how later revelations supersede earlier ones and the implications for hadith transmission.

summaries.bn: এই হাদিসটি ইমাম সাদিকের (আ.) সাহাবী মানসুর ইবনে হাযিম এবং ইমামের মধ্যে একটি কথোপকথন বর্ণনা করে। রাবী জিজ্ঞেস করেন কেন ইমাম বিভিন্ন লোককে একই প্রশ্নের ভিন্ন ভিন্ন উত্তর দেন। ইমাম ব্যাখ্যা করেন যে তাঁর উত্তরগুলো প্রতিটি প্রশ্নকারীর আধ্যাত্মিক ও বৌদ্ধিক সক্ষমতা অনুযায়ী ভিন্ন হয়। তারপর নবীর সাহাবীদের মধ্যে স্পষ্ট বিরোধাভাস নাসখের মতবাদ দিয়ে ব্যাখ্যা করা হয়।
summaries.de: Dieser Hadith überliefert einen Dialog zwischen Mansur ibn Hazim, einem Gefährten des Imam al-Sadiq (as), und dem Imam selbst. Der Erzähler fragt, warum der Imam verschiedenen Personen auf dieselbe Frage unterschiedliche Antworten gibt. Der Imam erklärt, dass seine Antworten je nach geistiger und intellektueller Kapazität des Fragenden variieren. Die scheinbaren Widersprüche unter den Gefährten des Propheten werden dann durch die Lehre der Abrogation (Naskh) erklärt: Der Prophet selbst gab manchmal auf dieselbe Frage zu verschiedenen Zeiten unterschiedliche Antworten, wobei spätere Antworten frühere aufhoben.
summaries.en: This hadith records a dialogue between Mansur ibn Hazim, a companion of Imam al-Sadiq (AS), and the Imam himself. The narrator asks why the Imam gives different answers to different questioners asking the same question. The Imam explains that his answers vary according to the spiritual and intellectual capacity of each questioner — a pedagogical principle of tailored guidance. The narrator then asks about apparent contradictions among the companions of the Prophet, and the Imam explains this through the doctrine of abrogation (naskh): the Prophet himself sometimes gave different answers to the same question at different times, with later revelations or rulings superseding earlier ones, causing apparent contradictions among honest narrators who heard the Prophet at different times.
summaries.es: Este hadith registra un diálogo entre Mansur ibn Hazim, compañero del Imam al-Sadiq (AS), y el Imam mismo. El narrador pregunta por qué el Imam da diferentes respuestas a diferentes personas que hacen la misma pregunta. El Imam explica que sus respuestas varían según la capacidad espiritual e intelectual de cada preguntante. Luego, las aparentes contradicciones entre los compañeros del Profeta se explican a través de la doctrina de la abrogación (naskh): el propio Profeta a veces daba diferentes respuestas a la misma pregunta en distintos momentos, con respuestas posteriores que abrogaban las anteriores.
summaries.fa: این حدیث گفتگویی را میان منصور بن حازم، از اصحاب امام صادق علیه السلام، و امام بیان می‌کند. راوی می‌پرسد چرا امام به سائلان مختلف جواب‌های متفاوت می‌دهد. امام توضیح می‌دهد که پاسخ‌ها بر اساس ظرفیت و استعداد هر فرد تنظیم می‌شوند. سپس اختلاف در احادیث اصحاب پیامبر با اصل نسخ توضیح داده می‌شود: خود پیامبر در زمان‌های مختلف جواب‌های متفاوتی می‌داد که برخی احادیث، احادیث قبلی را نسخ می‌کردند.
summaries.fr: Ce hadith rapporte un dialogue entre Mansur ibn Hazim, un compagnon de l'Imam al-Sadiq (AS), et l'Imam lui-même. Le narrateur demande pourquoi l'Imam donne des réponses différentes à des personnes différentes posant la même question. L'Imam explique que ses réponses varient selon la capacité spirituelle et intellectuelle de chaque questionneur. Les contradictions apparentes entre les compagnons du Prophète sont ensuite expliquées par la doctrine de l'abrogation (naskh) : le Prophète lui-même donnait parfois des réponses différentes à la même question à des moments distincts, les réponses ultérieures abrogeant les précédentes.
summaries.id: Hadis ini merekam dialog antara Mansur ibn Hazim, sahabat Imam al-Sadiq (AS), dengan sang Imam. Perawi bertanya mengapa Imam memberikan jawaban berbeda kepada penanya yang berbeda atas pertanyaan yang sama. Imam menjelaskan bahwa jawabannya disesuaikan dengan kapasitas spiritual dan intelektual masing-masing penanya. Kemudian, perbedaan di antara para sahabat Nabi dijelaskan melalui doktrin nasakh: Nabi sendiri terkadang memberikan jawaban berbeda untuk pertanyaan yang sama pada waktu berbeda, dengan jawaban yang datang kemudian menasakh yang sebelumnya.
summaries.ru: Этот хадис передаёт диалог между Мансуром ибн Хазимом, сподвижником Имама ас-Садика (мир ему), и самим Имамом. Передатчик спрашивает, почему Имам даёт разным людям разные ответы на один и тот же вопрос. Имам объясняет, что его ответы варьируются в зависимости от духовной и интеллектуальной способности каждого спрашивающего. Затем видимые противоречия среди сподвижников Пророка объясняются через доктрину отмены (насх): сам Пророк иногда давал разные ответы на один вопрос в разное время, а последующие ответы отменяли предыдущие.
summaries.tr: Bu hadis, İmam Sadık'ın (a.s.) yoldaşı Mansur ibn Hazim ile İmam arasındaki bir diyaloğu aktarır. Râvi, İmam'ın farklı kişilere aynı soruyu farklı neden yanıtladığını sorar. İmam, cevaplarının her soranın ruhani ve entelektüel kapasitesine göre değiştiğini açıklar. Daha sonra, Peygamber'in sahabileri arasındaki görünür çelişkiler nesih (naskh) doktriniyle açıklanır: Peygamber'in kendisi farklı zamanlarda aynı soruya farklı yanıtlar vermiş, sonraki yanıtlar öncekilerini neshetmiştir.
summaries.ur: یہ حدیث امام صادق علیہ السلام کے صحابی منصور بن حازم اور امام کے درمیان ایک مکالمے کو بیان کرتی ہے۔ راوی پوچھتا ہے کہ امام مختلف لوگوں کو ایک ہی سوال کے مختلف جوابات کیوں دیتے ہیں۔ امام وضاحت کرتے ہیں کہ ان کے جوابات ہر سوال کرنے والے کی روحانی اور علمی استعداد کے مطابق ہوتے ہیں۔ پھر نبی کریم صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ کے اصحاب کے درمیان ظاہری اختلافات کو نسخ کے اصول سے سمجھایا گیا ہے: خود نبی نے مختلف اوقات میں مختلف جوابات دیے، بعد والے جوابات پہلے والوں کو منسوخ کرتے تھے۔
summaries.zh: 这则圣训记录了伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）的弟子曼苏尔·本·哈齐姆与伊玛目之间的对话。叙述者询问为何伊玛目对不同人就同一问题给出不同答案。伊玛目解释说，他的答案因每个提问者的精神和智识能力不同而有所差异——这是因材施教的教育原则。然后，先知圣门弟子之间的表面矛盾通过废止（نسخ）教义得到解释：先知本人有时在不同时间对同一问题给出不同答案，后来的启示或判决废止了之前的，从而导致诚实的叙述者之间出现表面矛盾。

translation.ur: تو آپ مجھے جواب دیتے ہیں
تو آپ اسے جواب دیتے ہیں

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (216/ 1)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا بَالِي أَسْأَلُكَ عَنِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَتُجِيبُنِي فِيهَا بِالْجَوَابِ ثُمَّ يَجِيئُكَ غَيْرِي فَتُجِيبُهُ فِيهَا بِجَوَابٍ آخَرَ فَقَالَ إِنَّا نُجِيبُ النَّاسَ عَلَى الزِّيَادَةِ وَ النُّقْصَانِ

قَالَ قُلْتُ فَأَخْبِرْنِي عَنْ أَصْحَابِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) صَدَقُوا عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) أَمْ كَذَبُوا قَالَ بَلْ صَدَقُوا قَالَ قُلْتُ فَمَا بَالُهُمُ اخْتَلَفُوا فَقَالَ أَ مَا تَعْلَمُ أَنَّ الرَّجُلَ كَانَ يَأْتِي رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَيَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الْمَسْأَلَةِ فَيُجِيبُهُ فِيهَا بِالْجَوَابِ ثُمَّ يُجِيبُهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ مَا يَنْسَخُ ذَلِكَ الْجَوَابَ فَنَسَخَتِ الْأَحَادِيثُ بَعْضُهَا بَعْضاً .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Najran, from Aasim Bin Humeyd, from Mansour Bin Hazim who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is the matter that I ask you<sup>asws</sup> the question, so you<sup>asws</sup> answer me with regards to it with the answer, then there comes to you other than me, so you<sup>asws</sup> answer him with another answer, regarding his question?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘We<sup>asws</sup> answer the people sometimes more and sometimes less (as per their merit/status)’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘I said, ‘So inform me about the companions of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>. Were they truthful upon Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> or they lied (while reporting)?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But they (generally) were truthful’. I said, ‘So what is the matter with them that they are differing?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Do you not know that the man would come to Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, and he would ask him<sup>saww</sup> the question, so he<sup>saww</sup> would answer him with regards to it with an answer. Then he<sup>saww</sup> would answer him after that what Abrogated that answer. So the Ahadeeth got Abrogated by the other (Ahadeeth)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu Najran from ‘Asim ibn Humayd from Mansur ibn Hazim who has said the following. “I said to (Imam) abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.), ‘It is confuses me that when I ask you a question and you give an answer and then other person comes and you give a different answer for the same question.’” The Imam replied, “We answer people in a larger and reduced forms.” I then asked, “Did the Sahabah, companions of the holy Prophet speak the truth or lies when narrating his Hadith?” The Imam replied, “They spoke the truth.” I then said, “Why then they have differences?” Have you not considered the fact that a man would come to the holy Prophet (s.a.) and ask a question and would give him an answer and then he would answer that would abrogate the previous answer. Thus, Ahadith abrogated other Ahadith.”

translations.bn: আলী ইবনে মুহাম্মাদ, সাহল ইবনে যিয়াদ থেকে, তিনি ইবনে মাহবুব থেকে, তিনি আলী ইবনে রি'আব থেকে, তিনি আবু উবাইদাহ থেকে, তিনি আবু জা'ফার আলাইহিস সালাম থেকে বর্ণনা করেছেন যে তিনি বলেছেন:
translations.de: Ali ibn Muhammad, von Sahl ibn Ziyad, von Ibn Mahbub, von Ali ibn Ri'ab, von Abu Ubayda, von Abu Ja'far (Friede sei mit ihm), der sagte:
translations.en: Ali ibn Muhammad, from Sahl ibn Ziyad, from Ibn Mahbub, from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Abu Ubayda, from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) who said:
translations.es: Ali ibn Muhammad, de Sahl ibn Ziyad, de Ibn Mahbub, de Ali ibn Ri'ab, de Abu Ubayda, de Abu Ja'far (la paz sea con él) que dijo:
translations.fa: علی بن محمد از سهل بن زیاد، از ابن محبوب، از علی بن رئاب، از ابوعبیده، از ابوجعفر علیه السلام روایت کرده که فرمود:
translations.fr: Ali ibn Muhammad, de Sahl ibn Ziyad, d'Ibn Mahbub, d'Ali ibn Ri'ab, d'Abu Ubayda, d'Abu Ja'far (que la paix soit sur lui) qui dit :
translations.id: Ali bin Muhammad, dari Sahl bin Ziyad, dari Ibnu Mahbub, dari Ali bin Ri'ab, dari Abu Ubayda, dari Abu Ja'far (alaihis salam) yang bersabda:
translations.ru: Али ибн Мухаммад, от Сахля ибн Зийада, от Ибн Махбуба, от Али ибн Риаба, от Абу Убайды, от Абу Джа'фара (мир ему), который сказал:
translations.tr: Ali b. Muhammed, Sehl b. Ziyad'dan, o İbn Mahbub'dan, o Ali b. Riab'dan, o Ebu Ubeyde'den, o Ebu Cafer'den (a.s.) rivayet etmiştir ki buyurdu:
translations.ur: علی بن محمد نے سہل بن زیاد سے، انہوں نے ابن محبوب سے، انہوں نے علی بن رئاب سے، انہوں نے ابو عبیدہ سے، انہوں نے ابو جعفر علیہ السلام سے روایت کی کہ فرمایا:
translations.zh: 阿里·本·穆罕默德，从萨赫勒·本·兹亚德，从伊本·马赫布布，从阿里·本·里阿布，从阿布·欧拜达，从阿布·贾法尔（愿主赐他平安）传述，他说：

translations.bn: ইমাম (আ.) আমাকে বললেন: 'হে যিয়াদ! তুমি কী বলবে যদি আমরা আমাদের অনুসরণকারীদের মধ্যে কোনো ব্যক্তিকে তাকিয়্যার কিছু বিষয়ে ফতোয়া দিই?'
translations.de: Der Imam (F) sagte zu mir: 'O Ziyad! Was würdest du sagen, wenn wir einem Mann von denen, die unserer Führung folgen, in einer Angelegenheit aus der Taqiyya eine Fatwa erteilten?'
translations.en: He (the Imam) said to me: 'O Ziyad! What would you say if we were to issue a fatwa to a man from those who follow our authority, with something from taqiyya?'
translations.es: El Imam (p.b.u.h.) me dijo: '¡Oh Ziyad! ¿Qué dirías si emitiéramos una fatwa a un hombre de los que siguen nuestra autoridad, con algo de la taqiyya?'
translations.fa: امام به من فرمود: 'ای زیاد! اگر ما به مردی از کسانی که ولایت ما را می‌پذیرند، در مورد چیزی از تقیه فتوا دهیم، چه می‌گویی؟'
translations.fr: L'Imam (p.b.u.h.) me dit : 'Ô Ziyad ! Que dirais-tu si nous émettions une fatwa à un homme parmi ceux qui suivent notre autorité, avec quelque chose de la taqiyya ?'
translations.id: Imam (a.s.) berkata kepadaku: 'Wahai Ziyad! Apa yang akan kamu katakan jika kami mengeluarkan fatwa kepada seorang laki-laki dari mereka yang mengikuti kepemimpinan kami, dengan sesuatu dari taqiyya?'
translations.ru: Имам (мир ему) сказал мне: 'О Зийад! Что бы ты сказал, если бы мы вынесли фетву мужчине из тех, кто следует нашей власти, касательно чего-либо из такийи?'
translations.tr: İmam (a.s.) bana dedi ki: 'Ey Ziyad! Velayetimize bağlı olan bir kimseye takiyyeden bir şey hakkında fetva verseydik ne derdin?'
translations.ur: امام علیہ السلام نے مجھ سے فرمایا: 'اے زیاد! تم کیا کہتے اگر ہم کسی ایسے شخص کو جو ہماری ولایت قبول کرتا ہو، تقیہ میں سے کسی چیز پر فتویٰ دیں؟'
translations.zh: 伊玛目（愿主赐他平安）对我说：'呀，兹亚德！如果我们给一个跟随我们领导的人在塔基亚方面颁布一条法令，你会怎么说？'

translations.bn: আবু উবাইদাহ বললেন: আমি তাঁকে (ইমামকে) বললাম: 'আপনি বেশি জানেন, আমি আপনার জন্য উৎসর্গিত হই।'
translations.de: Abu Ubayda sagte: Ich sagte zu ihm (dem Imam): 'Sie wissen es besser, möge ich für Sie geopfert werden.'
translations.en: He (Abu Ubayda) said: I said to him (the Imam): 'You are more knowing, may I be sacrificed for you.'
translations.es: Abu Ubayda dijo: Le dije al Imam: 'Vos sois el más sabio, que yo sea sacrificado por vos.'
translations.fa: ابوعبیده گفت: به او عرض کردم: 'شما داناترید، فدایت شوم.'
translations.fr: Abu Ubayda dit : Je lui dis (à l'Imam) : 'Vous êtes le plus savant, que je sois sacrifié pour vous.'
translations.id: Abu Ubayda berkata: Aku berkata kepadanya (Imam): 'Anda lebih mengetahui, semoga aku menjadi tebusanmu.'
translations.ru: Абу Убайда сказал: Я сказал ему (Имаму): 'Вы знаете лучше, да буду я принесён в жертву ради вас.'
translations.tr: Ebu Ubeyde dedi: Ona (İmama) dedim ki: 'Siz daha iyi bilirsiniz, sana feda olayım.'
translations.ur: ابو عبیدہ نے کہا: میں نے امام سے عرض کیا: 'آپ بہتر جانتے ہیں، قربان جاؤں آپ پر۔'
translations.zh: 阿布·欧拜达说：我对他（伊玛目）说：'您更了解，愿我为您献身。'

translations.bn: ইমাম বললেন: 'যদি সে তা অনুসরণ করে, তবে তা তার জন্য উত্তম এবং বৃহত্তর পুরস্কারের কারণ।'
translations.de: Der Imam sagte: 'Wenn er danach handelt, ist es besser für ihn und mit größerem Lohn verbunden.'
translations.en: He (the Imam) said: 'If he acts upon it, it is better for him and of greater reward.'
translations.es: El Imam dijo: 'Si actúa conforme a ello, es mejor para él y de mayor recompensa.'
translations.fa: فرمود: 'اگر بدان عمل کند، برای او بهتر و پاداش بزرگ‌تری دارد.'
translations.fr: L'Imam dit : 'Si il agit selon cela, c'est meilleur pour lui et d'une plus grande récompense.'
translations.id: Imam bersabda: 'Jika ia mengikutinya, itu lebih baik baginya dan lebih besar pahalanya.'
translations.ru: Имам сказал: 'Если он последует ей, это лучше для него и с большим вознаграждением.'
translations.tr: İmam buyurdu: 'Eğer ona uyarsa, bu onun için daha hayırlı ve daha büyük ecirdir.'
translations.ur: امام نے فرمایا: 'اگر وہ اس پر عمل کرے تو اس کے لیے بہتر اور زیادہ اجر کا باعث ہے۔'
translations.zh: 伊玛目说：'如果他依此而行，那对他来说更好，并有更大的回报。'

translations.bn: এবং আরেকটি বর্ণনায়: 'যদি সে তা অনুসরণ করে, সে পুরস্কৃত হবে; আর যদি সে তা ত্যাগ করে, আল্লাহর কসম, সে পাপী হবে।'
translations.de: Und in einer anderen Überlieferung: 'Wenn er danach handelt, wird er belohnt; und wenn er es verlässt, bei Gott, sündigt er.'
translations.en: And in another report: 'If he acts upon it, he will be rewarded; and if he abandons it, by God, he sins.'
translations.es: Y en otra narración: 'Si actúa conforme a ello, será recompensado; y si lo abandona, ¡por Dios, peca!'
translations.fa: و در روایتی دیگر: 'اگر بدان عمل کند، پاداش می‌گیرد؛ و اگر آن را ترک کند، به خدا گناه کرده است.'
translations.fr: Et dans une autre narration : 'S'il agit selon cela, il sera récompensé ; et s'il l'abandonne, par Dieu, il pèche.'
translations.id: Dan dalam riwayat lain: 'Jika ia mengikutinya, ia mendapat pahala; dan jika ia meninggalkannya, demi Allah, ia berdosa.'
translations.ru: И в другом сообщении: 'Если он последует ей, он будет вознаграждён; а если оставит это, клянусь Аллахом, он согрешит.'
translations.tr: Bir başka rivayette ise: 'Eğer ona uyarsa ecir alır; eğer onu terk ederse, Allah'a yemin olsun, günah işler.'
translations.ur: اور ایک دوسری روایت میں ہے: 'اگر وہ اس پر عمل کرے تو اجر پائے گا، اور اگر اسے چھوڑ دے تو خدا کی قسم گناہگار ہوگا۔'
translations.zh: 在另一个传述中：'如果他依此而行，他将获得回报；如果他放弃，以安拉起誓，他将犯罪。'

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: عَلِيُّ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي عُبَيْدَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قَالَ لِي يَا زِيَادُ مَا تَقُولُ لَوْ أَفْتَيْنَا رَجُلًا مِمَّنْ يَتَوَلَّانَا بِشَيْءٍ مِنَ التَّقِيَّةِ قَالَ قُلْتُ لَهُ أَنْتَ أَعْلَمُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ قَالَ إِنْ أَخَذَ بِهِ فَهُوَ خَيْرٌ لَهُ وَأَعْظَمُ أَجْرًا وَفِي رِوَايَةٍ أُخْرَى إِنْ أَخَذَ بِهِ أُوجِرَ وَإِنْ تَرَكَهُ وَاللَّهِ أَثِمَ

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Ali ibn Muhammad ibn Bundaar or Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qasaani, common transmitters in al-Kulayni's chains
narrators.known_identity: Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qasaani (a common transmitter in al-Kafi's chains)
Sahl ibn Ziyad al-Adami al-Razi, a transmitter of the 3rd century AH. His reliability was debated among scholars.
al-Hasan ibn Mahbub al-Sarrad (d. 224 AH), one of the most reliable narrators in Shia hadith literature
Ali ibn Ri'ab al-Kufi, a trusted companion of Imam al-Sadiq and Imam al-Kazim (AS)

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Abu Ubayda al-Hadhdha' (Ziyad ibn Abi Raja'), a companion of Imam al-Baqir and al-Sadiq (AS)
narrators.known_identity: Abu Ubayda al-Hadhdha', companion of the Imams
Imam Muhammad al-Baqir (AS), the fifth Imam (d. 114 AH)

key_phrases.phrase_en: May I be sacrificed for you
You are more knowing
From those who follow our authority (wilaya)

bn.التَّقِيَّة: তাকিয়্যা — সতর্কতামূলক গোপনীয়তা; বিপদের সময় বিশ্বাস বা অনুশীলন লুকানো

de.أَثِمَ: Er sündigt — begeht eine geistliche Sünde durch Verlassen der Direktive des Imams
de.التَّقِيَّة: Taqiyya — vorsorgende Verstellung; Verbergung von Glauben oder Praxis unter Zwang

en.أَثِمَ: He sins — incurs spiritual sin for abandoning the Imam's directive
en.أَفْتَيْنَا: We issued a fatwa — legal ruling; the plural 'we' is the royal/majestic plural used by the Imam
en.أُوجِرَ: He will be rewarded — rewarded by God for compliance with the Imam's guidance
en.التَّقِيَّة: Taqiyya — precautionary dissimulation; a legal practice in Shia jurisprudence permitting concealment of one's true beliefs or practices under duress or danger
en.يَتَوَلَّانَا: Those who follow our authority (wilaya) — Shia believers who accept the Imam's religious and political guardianship

es.أَثِمَ: Peca — incurre en pecado espiritual por abandonar la directiva del Imam
es.التَّقِيَّة: Taqiyya — disimulación preventiva; ocultamiento de las creencias o prácticas bajo coacción

fa.التَّقِيَّة: تقیه — پنهان‌سازی احتیاطی؛ پنهان داشتن عقیده یا عمل در شرایط خطر

fr.أَثِمَ: Il pèche — encourt un péché spirituel en abandonnant la directive de l'Imam
fr.التَّقِيَّة: Taqiyya — dissimulation préventive ; dissimuler ses croyances ou pratiques en cas de danger

id.التَّقِيَّة: Taqiyya — penyembunyian preventif; menyembunyikan keyakinan atau praktik dalam kondisi bahaya

ru.أَثِمَ: Он согрешит — несёт духовный грех за отказ от директивы Имама
ru.التَّقِيَّة: Такийя — превентивная диссимуляция; сокрытие убеждений или практик в опасной ситуации

tr.أَثِمَ: Günah işler — İmam'ın direktifini terk etmek
tr.التَّقِيَّة: Takiyye — koruyucu gizleme; Şii fıkhında baskı altında inancını veya pratiğini saklama

ur.أَثِمَ: گناہگار ہونا — امام کی ہدایت کو چھوڑنے پر روحانی گناہ
ur.التَّقِيَّة: تقیہ — احتیاطی خفیہ عمل؛ شیعہ فقہ میں خطرے کے وقت عقیدے یا عمل کو چھپانا

zh.أَثِمَ: 犯罪——因放弃伊玛目指令而犯下的精神罪
zh.التَّقِيَّة: 塔基亚——预防性掩饰；在危险下隐藏信仰或做法

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম বাকির তাকিয়্যা অনুসরণের পুরস্কার সম্পর্কে কী বলেছেন?
seo_questions.de: Was sagt Imam al-Baqir über die Belohnung für das Befolgen der Taqiyya?
seo_questions.en: What does Imam al-Baqir say about the reward for following taqiyya in Al-Kafi?
seo_questions.es: ¿Qué dice el Imam al-Baqir sobre la recompensa por seguir la taqiyya en Al-Kafi?
seo_questions.fa: امام باقر درباره حکم تقیه و پاداش آن چه فرمودند؟
seo_questions.fr: Que dit l'Imam al-Baqir sur la récompense de la taqiyya dans Al-Kafi ?
