Narrations about the principle that the later statement of an Imam abrogates or supersedes the earlier one
Hadiths where Imam al-Sadiq approves a companion's answer with the supplication رحمك الله

summaries.bn: ইমাম আল-সাদিক (আ.) একজন সাথীর কাছে জিজ্ঞেস করেন যে দুটি পরস্পরবিরোধী বর্ণনার মধ্যে কোনটি অনুসরণ করবে। সাথী সর্বশেষটি অনুসরণের কথা বলেন, এবং ইমাম দোয়া দিয়ে এই উত্তর অনুমোদন করেন। এই হাদীস শিয়া হাদীস পদ্ধতিতে পরবর্তী বর্ণনার অগ্রাধিকারের নীতি প্রতিষ্ঠা করে।
summaries.de: Imam al-Sadiq (F) stellt einem Gefährten eine hypothetische Frage: wenn der Imam zu verschiedenen Zeiten zwei widersprechende Hadithe überliefert, welchem soll man folgen? Der Gefährte antwortet, er würde dem neueren folgen, und der Imam billigt diese Antwort mit einem Gebet. Dieser Hadith begründet das Prinzip der chronologischen Präferenz in der schiitischen Hadithlehre.
summaries.en: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) poses a hypothetical question to a companion: if the Imam gives two contradictory narrations at different times, which should be followed? The companion answers he would follow the more recent one, and the Imam approves with a supplication of mercy. This hadith establishes the principle of tarjih (preference) by recency — the later narration abrogates or supersedes the earlier one in cases of apparent contradiction, a fundamental principle in Shia hadith methodology.
summaries.es: El Imam al-Sadiq (P) plantea una pregunta hipotética a un compañero: si el Imam da dos narraciones contradictorias en diferentes momentos, ¿cuál debe seguirse? El compañero responde que seguiría la más reciente, y el Imam aprueba con una súplica. Este hadith establece el principio de preferencia por cronología — la narración más reciente tiene precedencia sobre la anterior en casos de contradicción aparente.
summaries.fa: امام صادق علیه السلام از یک صحابی می‌پرسد که اگر دو روایت متعارض در زمان‌های مختلف صادر شود، کدام را باید برگزید. صحابی پاسخ می‌دهد که روایت اخیر را، و امام با دعا این پاسخ را تأیید می‌کند. این حدیث اصل «ترجیح اخیر» را در فقه شیعه بنیاد می‌گذارد.
summaries.fr: L'Imam al-Sadiq (P) pose une question hypothétique à un compagnon : si l'Imam donne deux narrations contradictoires à des moments différents, laquelle doit-on suivre ? Le compagnon répond qu'il suivrait la plus récente, et l'Imam approuve par une supplication. Ce hadith établit le principe de préférence chronologique — la narration la plus récente prévaut sur l'antérieure en cas de contradiction apparente.
summaries.id: Imam al-Shadiq (as) mengajukan pertanyaan hipotetis kepada seorang sahabat: jika Imam memberikan dua narasi yang bertentangan pada waktu yang berbeda, mana yang harus diikuti? Sahabat menjawab mengikuti yang terbaru, dan Imam menyetujui dengan doa. Hadits ini menetapkan prinsip tarjih berdasarkan kronologis — narasi terbaru menggantikan yang lebih lama jika ada kontradiksi.
summaries.ru: Имам ас-Садик (мир ему) задаёт сподвижнику гипотетический вопрос: если Имам в разное время передаёт два противоречивых хадиса, какому из них следовать? Сподвижник отвечает, что последнему, и Имам одобряет ответ молитвой. Этот хадис устанавливает принцип приоритета хронологически позднейшей передачи в шиитской хадисной науке.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık (a.s.) bir sahabeye farklı zamanlarda verilen iki çelişkili rivayetten hangisinin tercih edileceğini sorar. Sahabe sonrakinin tercih edileceğini söyler, İmam bu cevabı dua ile tasdik eder. Bu hadis, Şia hadis metodolojisinde çelişkili rivayetlerde en son rivayetin öncelikli olduğu prensibini ortaya koymaktadır.
summaries.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام نے ایک صحابی سے سوال کیا کہ اگر دو متضاد روایات ہوں تو کونسی اختیار کریں۔ صحابی نے جواب دیا کہ آخری روایت، امام نے دعا سے اس جواب کی تصویب کی۔ یہ حدیث حدیثی اصول کا اہم پہلو بیان کرتی ہے: جب دو روایات میں تضاد ہو تو بعد کی روایت کو اولیت حاصل ہے۔
summaries.zh: 伊玛目萨迪格（愿主赐他平安）向一位同伴提出假设性问题：如果伊玛目在不同时间传述两段相矛盾的圣训，应该遵循哪一个？同伴回答说遵循最新的，伊玛目以祈祷表示认可。此圣训确立了什叶派圣训学中的时间优先原则——在表面矛盾的情况下，较新的传述优先于较早的传述。

translation.en: what is your view / tell me

translation.ur: اور میں نے تمہیں حدیث سنائی

translation.en: may He have mercy on you

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (220/ 1)
text: أَ رَأَيْتَكَ لَوْ حَدَّثْتُكَ بِحَدِيثٍ الْعَامَ ثُمَّ جِئْتَنِي مِنْ قَابِلٍ فَحَدَّثْتُكَ بِخِلَافِهِ بِأَيِّهِمَا كُنْتَ تَأْخُذُ قَالَ قُلْتُ كُنْتُ آخُذُ بِالْأَخِيرِ فَقَالَ لِي رَحِمَكَ اللَّهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Usman Bin Isa, from Al Husayn Bin Al Mukhtar, from one of our companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘What is your view if I<sup>asws</sup> was to narrate to you with the general Hadeeth, then you come to me in the future, so I<sup>asws</sup> narrate to you with one different to it. Which of the two would you be taking by?’ He (the narrator) said, ‘I said. ‘We would take the latest one’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said to me: ‘May Allah<sup>azwj</sup> have Mercy on you’.
en.sarwar: 8. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ‘Uthman ibn ‘Isa from al-Husayn ibn al-Mukhtar from certain persons of our people from abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) who has said the following. “If you would come to me for an answer to a question and I give you an answer of a general nature and then you come again and I give you an opposite answer which one would you follow?” I replied, “I would follow the later.” The Imam (a.s.) said, “May Allah grant you blessings.”

translations.bn: এবং তার থেকে, তার পিতার থেকে, ইসমাঈল ইবনে মাররার থেকে, ইউনুস থেকে, দাউদ ইবনে ফারকাদ থেকে, আল-মুআল্লা ইবনে খুনাইস থেকে যিনি বলেছেন:
translations.de: Und von ihm, von seinem Vater, von Ismail ibn Marrar, von Yunus, von Dawood ibn Farqad, von al-Mualla ibn Khunays, der sagte:
translations.en: And from him, from his father, from Ismail ibn Marrar, from Yunus, from Dawood ibn Farqad, from al-Mualla ibn Khunays who said:
translations.es: Y de él, de su padre, de Ismail ibn Marrar, de Yunus, de Dawood ibn Farqad, de al-Mualla ibn Khunays quien dijo:
translations.fa: و از او، از پدرش، از اسماعیل بن مرار، از یونس، از داوود بن فرقد، از المعلی بن خنیس که گفت:
translations.fr: Et de lui, de son père, d'Ismail ibn Marrar, de Yunus, de Dawood ibn Farqad, d'al-Mualla ibn Khunays qui dit :
translations.id: Dan darinya, dari ayahnya, dari Ismail bin Marrar, dari Yunus, dari Dawood bin Farqad, dari al-Mualla bin Khunays yang berkata:
translations.ru: И от него, от его отца, от Исмаила ибн Маррара, от Юнуса, от Дауда ибн Фаркада, от аль-Муаллы ибн Хунайса, который сказал:
translations.tr: Ondan, babasından, İsmail b. Merrar'dan, Yunus'tan, Davud b. Farkad'dan, şöyle diyen el-Mualla b. Huneys'ten:
translations.ur: اور ان سے، ان کے والد سے، اسماعیل بن مرار سے، یونس سے، داؤد بن فرقد سے، المعلی بن خنیس سے جنہوں نے کہا:
translations.zh: 从他，从他的父亲，从伊斯玛仪·本·马拉尔，从优努斯，从达乌德·本·法尔卡德，从穆阿拉·本·胡奈斯传述，后者说：

translations.bn: আমি আবু আবদিল্লাহ আলাইহিস সালামকে বললাম: যখন তোমাদের পূর্ববর্তীদের থেকে একটি হাদিস আসে এবং তোমাদের পরবর্তীদের থেকে একটি হাদিস আসে, তাদের দুটির কোনটি আমরা গ্রহণ করব? তখন তিনি বললেন: এটি গ্রহণ করতে থাকো যতক্ষণ না জীবিত (ইমাম) এর কাছ থেকে তোমাদের কাছে পৌঁছায়; তাই যদি জীবিতের কাছ থেকে তোমাদের কাছে পৌঁছায়, তখন তার কথায় অনুসরণ করো।
translations.de: Ich sagte zu Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm): Wenn ein Hadith von euren Früheren und ein Hadith von euren Späteren kommt, welchem von beiden sollen wir folgen? Da sagte er: Haltet daran fest, bis euch (eine Aussage) vom Lebenden (Imam) erreicht; wenn euch dann vom Lebenden etwas erreicht, nehmt seine Worte.
translations.en: I said to Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him): When there comes a hadith from your first ones and a hadith from your last ones, by which of the two should we act? So he said: Adhere to it until there reaches you from the living one; then if there does reach you from the living one, take his words.
translations.es: Dije a Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él): Cuando viene un hadith de vuestros primeros y un hadith de vuestros últimos, ¿cuál de los dos debemos tomar? Entonces dijo: Seguidlo hasta que os llegue del viviente (Imam); entonces si os llega del viviente, tomad sus palabras.
translations.fa: به ابوعبدالله علیه السلام گفتم: وقتی حدیثی از اولین شما و حدیثی از آخرین شما می‌آید، به کدام یک عمل کنیم؟ فرمود: به آن عمل کنید تا از امام زنده به شما برسد؛ پس اگر از امام زنده به شما رسید، به گفتار او عمل کنید.
translations.fr: J'ai dit à Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui) : Quand vient un hadith de vos premiers et un hadith de vos derniers, lequel des deux devons-nous prendre ? Il dit alors : Tenez-vous-y jusqu'à ce que vous parvienne (la parole) du vivant (l'Imam) ; puis si vous parvient (la parole) du vivant, prenez sa parole.
translations.id: Aku berkata kepada Abu Abdillah alaihis salam: Ketika datang sebuah hadith dari yang terdahulu dari kalian dan sebuah hadith dari yang terakhir dari kalian, yang manakah dari keduanya yang harus kami ambil? Maka beliau bersabda: Berpeganglah dengannya hingga sampai kepada kalian dari yang hidup (Imam); maka jika sampai kepada kalian dari yang hidup, ambillah perkataannya.
translations.ru: Я сказал Абу Абдиллаху (мир ему): Когда приходит хадис от ваших первых и хадис от ваших последних, которому из двух нам следовать? Он сказал: Придерживайтесь его, пока до вас не дойдёт (слово) от живого (Имама); тогда если до вас дойдёт от живого, берите его слова.
translations.tr: Ebu Abdullah'a (a.s.) dedim ki: Öncekilerinizden bir hadis ve sonrakilerinizden bir hadis geldiğinde, hangisini esas almalıyız? Buyurdu: Yaşayan (İmam)'dan size ulaşana kadar ona sarılın; yaşayan (İmam)'dan size ulaştığında ise onun sözünü alın.
translations.ur: میں نے ابو عبد اللہ علیہ السلام سے کہا: جب آپ کے پہلوں کی ایک حدیث آئے اور آپ کے آخر والوں کی ایک حدیث آئے، تو ہم ان میں سے کس پر عمل کریں؟ آپ نے فرمایا: اس پر عمل کرتے رہو یہاں تک کہ تم تک زندہ (امام) کی (بات) پہنچے؛ پس جب تمہیں زندہ (امام) سے (بات) پہنچے تو ان کے قول پر عمل کرو۔
translations.zh: 我对阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）说：当你们前辈来一段圣训，而你们后辈也来一段圣训，我们应当遵从哪一个呢？他回答说：遵从它，直到有来自在世（伊玛目）的（话语）传达到你们；那么如果从在世的（伊玛目）传达到你们，就遵从他的话语。

translations.bn: বর্ণনাকারী বললেন: তারপর আবু আবদিল্লাহ আলাইহিস সালাম বললেন: আমরা, আল্লাহর কসম, তোমাদের এমন কিছুতে প্রবেশ করাই না যা তোমাদের জন্য সহজ নয়।
translations.de: Er (der Erzähler) sagte: Dann sagte Abu Abdillah (Friede sei mit ihm): Wir, bei Gott, führen euch nicht in etwas, außer was für euch leicht ist.
translations.en: He (the narrator) said: Then Abu Abdillah (peace be upon him) said: We, by God, do not lead you into anything except what is within your capacity.
translations.es: Él (el narrador) dijo: Luego Abu Abdillah (la paz sea con él) dijo: Nosotros, por Dios, no os introducimos sino en aquello que esté a vuestro alcance.
translations.fa: راوی گفت: سپس ابوعبدالله علیه السلام فرمود: ما، به خدا، شما را وارد چیزی نمی‌کنیم مگر آنچه برای شما میسر است.
translations.fr: Il (le narrateur) dit : Puis Abu Abdillah (que la paix soit sur lui) dit : Nous, par Dieu, ne vous engageons que dans ce qui est à votre portée.
translations.id: Perawi berkata: Kemudian Abu Abdillah alaihis salam bersabda: Kami, demi Allah, tidak memasukkan kalian ke dalam sesuatu kecuali apa yang lapang bagi kalian.
translations.ru: Он (рассказчик) сказал: Затем Абу Абдиллах (мир ему) сказал: Мы, клянусь Аллахом, не вводим вас ни во что, кроме того, что вам под силу.
translations.tr: Ravi dedi: Sonra Ebu Abdullah (a.s.) buyurdu: Biz, Allah'a yemin olsun, sizi ancak gücünüzün yeteceği şeye sokarız.
translations.ur: راوی نے کہا: پھر ابو عبد اللہ علیہ السلام نے فرمایا: ہم، اللہ کی قسم، تمہیں کسی ایسی چیز میں نہیں ڈالتے جو تمہاری طاقت سے باہر ہو۔
translations.zh: 他（传述者）说：然后阿布·阿卜杜拉（愿主赐他平安）说：我们，以真主起誓，不引导你们进入任何对你们来说非宽裕的事情。

translations.bn: এবং অন্য একটি বর্ণনায়: সবচেয়ে নতুনটি গ্রহণ করো।
translations.de: Und in einem anderen Bericht: Nehmt das Neueste.
translations.en: And in another report: Take the most recent.
translations.es: Y en otro relato: Tomad lo más reciente.
translations.fa: و در روایتی دیگر: جدیدترین را بگیرید.
translations.fr: Et dans un autre récit : Prenez le plus récent.
translations.id: Dan dalam riwayat lain: Ambillah yang terbaru.
translations.ru: И в другом предании: Берите самое последнее.
translations.tr: Başka bir rivayette ise: En yenisini alın.
translations.ur: اور ایک دوسری روایت میں: سب سے جدید (حدیث) لو۔
translations.zh: 而在另一则传述中：你们遵循最新的。

diacritics_changes: Added complete tashkeel to the full text

isnad_matn.isnad_ar: وَ عَنْهُ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ مَرَّارٍ عَنْ يُونُسَ عَنْ دَاوُدَ بْنِ فَرْقَدٍ عَنِ الْمُعَلَّى بْنِ خُنَيْسٍ قَالَ
isnad_matn.matn_ar: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِذَا جَاءَ حَدِيثٌ عَنْ أَوَّلِكُمْ وَ حَدِيثٌ عَنْ آخِرِكُمْ بِأَيِّهِمَا نَأْخُذُ فَقَالَ خُذُوا بِهِ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَكُمْ عَنِ الْحَيِّ فَإِنْ بَلَغَكُمْ عَنِ الْحَيِّ فَخُذُوا بِقَوْلِهِ قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلَامُ إِنَّا وَ اللَّهِ لَا نُدْخِلُكُمْ إِلَّا فِيمَا يَسَعُكُمْ وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ خُذُوا بِالْأَحْدَثِ

narrators.known_identity: Ismail ibn Marrar, a narrator in the chains of Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman

narrators.ambiguity_note: Likely Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman, prominent narrator of al-Kafi
narrators.known_identity: Yunus ibn Abd al-Rahman
Dawood ibn Farqad, companion of Imam al-Sadiq (a.s.)
al-Mualla ibn Khunays, trusted companion of Imam al-Sadiq (a.s.) who was martyred
Imam Ja'far ibn Muhammad al-Sadiq (a.s.), the sixth Imam

key_phrases.phrase_en: from the living one (the Imam)
Take the most recent
We do not lead you into anything except what is within your capacity

bn.الحيّ: জীবিত — বর্তমান ইমাম যিনি সমসাময়িক নির্দেশনার সর্বোচ্চ কর্তৃত্ব
bn.بالأحدث: সবচেয়ে নতুন — পরস্পরবিরোধী হাদিসে পরবর্তীটি অগ্রাধিকার পাবে
bn.يسعكم: তোমাদের জন্য সহজ — ইমাম নিশ্চিত করেন যে তাঁর নির্দেশনা কষ্টসাধ্য নয়

de.الحيّ: Der Lebende — der gegenwärtige Imam als höchste Autorität für zeitgenössische Führung
de.بالأحدث: Das Neueste — bei widersprüchlichen Überlieferungen spiegelt die spätere aktuellere Führung wider
de.يسعكم: Für euch leicht — der Imam versichert, dass seine Führung keine unzumutbare Last auferlegt

en.الْحَيّ: The living one — referring to the current/living Imam who is the supreme authority for present guidance
en.بِالْأَحْدَثِ: The most recent — in case of conflicting narrations, the later one reflects more updated guidance
en.يَسَعُكُمْ: What is within your capacity — the Imam assures followers that his guidance does not impose undue hardship

es.الحيّ: El viviente — el Imam actual que es la máxima autoridad para la guía contemporánea
es.بالأحدث: Lo más reciente — ante narraciones contradictorias, la posterior refleja una guía más actualizada
es.يسعكم: A vuestro alcance — el Imam asegura que su guía no impone una carga indebida

fa.الحيّ: زنده — امام حاضر که بالاترین مرجعیت برای هدایت معاصر است
fa.بالأحدث: جدیدترین — در روایات متعارض، روایت متأخر اولویت دارد
fa.يسعكم: برای شما میسر — امام اطمینان می‌دهد که هدایت او مشقت ایجاد نمی‌کند

fr.الحيّ: Le vivant — l'Imam actuel qui est l'autorité suprême pour la guidance contemporaine
fr.بالأحدث: Le plus récent — en cas de narrations contradictoires, la plus récente reflète une guidance actualisée
fr.يسعكم: À votre portée — l'Imam assure que sa guidance n'impose pas de fardeau excessif

id.الحيّ: Yang hidup — merujuk kepada Imam yang sedang hidup sebagai otoritas tertinggi
id.بالأحدث: Yang terbaru — dalam hadith yang bertentangan, yang lebih baru mencerminkan petunjuk terkini
id.يسعكم: Lapang bagi kalian — Imam meyakinkan bahwa ajarannya tidak memberatkan

ru.الحيّ: Живой — нынешний Имам как высший авторитет для современного руководства
ru.بالأحدث: Самое последнее — при противоречащих преданиях более позднее отражает обновлённое руководство
ru.يسعكم: Вам под силу — Имам заверяет, что его руководство не возлагает чрезмерного бремени

tr.الحيّ: Yaşayan — güncel rehberliğin otoriter kaynağı olan mevcut İmam
tr.بالأحدث: En yeni — çelişkili rivayetlerde daha sonraki olan önceliklidir
tr.يسعكم: Gücünüzün yeteceği — İmam, rehberliğinin aşırı bir yük getirmediğini güvence verir

ur.الحيّ: زندہ — موجودہ امام جو معاصر راہنمائی کا سب سے بڑا ذریعہ ہے
ur.بالأحدث: سب سے جدید — متضاد روایات میں بعد والی روایت ترجیح پاتی ہے
ur.يسعكم: جو تمہارے لیے آسان ہو — امام یقین دلاتے ہیں کہ ان کی راہنمائی میں مشقت نہیں

zh.الحيّ: 在世的 — 指当前在世的伊玛目，是当代指引的最高权威
zh.بالأحدث: 最新的 — 在矛盾的传述中，较新的反映了最新的指引
zh.يسعكم: 对你们来说宽裕的 — 伊玛目保证其指引不会施加不当的负担

seo_questions.bn: ইমাম সাদিকের মতে বিভিন্ন ইমামের পরস্পরবিরোধী হাদিস কীভাবে সমাধান করতে হবে?
seo_questions.de: Wie sollen widersprüchliche Hadithe verschiedener Imame gemäß Imam al-Sadiq aufgelöst werden?
seo_questions.en: How should conflicting hadiths from different Imams be resolved according to Imam al-Sadiq?
seo_questions.es: ¿Cómo resolver hadiths contradictorios de diferentes Imames según el Imam al-Sadiq?
seo_questions.fa: طبق امام صادق علیه السلام، چگونه با احادیث متعارض از ائمه مختلف برخورد کنیم؟
seo_questions.fr: Comment résoudre les hadiths contradictoires de différents Imams selon l'Imam al-Sadiq ?
seo_questions.id: Bagaimana menyelesaikan pertentangan antara hadith dari Imam-Imam yang berbeda menurut Imam al-Sadiq?
seo_questions.ru: Как следует разрешать противоречащие хадисы от разных Имамов согласно Имаму ас-Садику?
seo_questions.tr: İmam Sadık'a göre farklı İmamlardan gelen çelişkili hadisler nasıl çözülür?
seo_questions.ur: امام صادق علیہ السلام کے مطابق آئمہ کی متضاد احادیث کو کیسے حل کیا جائے؟
seo_questions.zh: 根据伊玛目萨迪克，应如何解决来自不同伊玛目的相互矛盾的圣训？

similar_content_hints.description: Other hadiths in al-Kafi's chapter on hadith differences that address the principle of preferring the more recent narration (al-akhadh bil-ahdat) when two contradictory hadiths exist.
Hadiths emphasizing the living Imam's authority as the ultimate arbiter for contemporary matters, including those that state the people should refer to the Imam of their time.
Hadiths in which the Imam assures followers that Islamic obligations do not exceed human capacity, echoing Quranic principles of no hardship in religion (la yukallifu Allahu nafsan illa wus'aha).

summaries.bn: ইমাম সাদিকের ঘনিষ্ঠ সঙ্গী আল-মুআল্লা ইবনে খুনাইস তাঁকে পূর্ববর্তী ও পরবর্তী ইমামদের পরস্পরবিরোধী বর্ণনা সমাধানের উপায় জিজ্ঞেস করলেন। ইমাম একটি মৌলিক নীতি স্থাপন করলেন: জীবিত (বর্তমান) ইমামের বর্ণনা অগ্রাধিকার পাবে, এবং অন্য বর্ণনায়: সবচেয়ে নতুন হাদিসটি নিন। এই হাদিস শিয়া উসূল আল-ফিকহে পরস্পরবিরোধী হাদিস সমাধানে মৌলিক গুরুত্ব বহন করে।
summaries.de: Al-Mualla ibn Khunays, ein enger Gefährte des Imam al-Sadiq, fragte ihn, wie widersprüchliche Überlieferungen von früheren und späteren Imamen aufgelöst werden sollen. Der Imam legte ein grundlegendes hermeneutisches Prinzip fest: Die Überlieferung vom lebenden (gegenwärtigen) Imam hat Vorrang; und in einer anderen Version: Nehmt die neueste Überlieferung. Dieser Hadith ist im schiitischen Usul al-Fiqh grundlegend für die Auflösung von Widersprüchen im Hadith-Korpus.
summaries.en: Al-Mualla ibn Khunays, a companion of Imam al-Sadiq, asked him how to resolve conflicting narrations from earlier and later Imams. The Imam's response establishes a key hermeneutical principle: the narration from the living (current) Imam takes precedence, and in another version of the same ruling, take the more recent narration. This hadith is foundational in Shia usul al-fiqh for resolving contradictions in the hadith corpus, with the living Imam representing the authoritative and updated source of guidance for contemporary believers.
summaries.es: Al-Mualla ibn Khunays, cercano compañero del Imam al-Sadiq, le preguntó cómo resolver las narraciones contradictorias de los Imames anteriores y posteriores. El Imam estableció un principio hermenéutico fundamental: la narración del Imam vivo (actual) tiene prioridad; y en otra versión: tomad la narración más reciente. Este hadith es fundamental en el usul al-fiqh chiíta para resolver contradicciones en el corpus de hadiths.
summaries.fa: معلی بن خنیس، یکی از یاران نزدیک امام صادق، از آن حضرت پرسید که چگونه میان روایات متعارض از ائمه پیشین و پسین جمع کند. امام اصلی بنیادی را بیان فرمود: روایت امام زنده (حاضر) مقدم است و در روایت دیگر: جدیدترین را بگیرید. این حدیث از مهم‌ترین اصول در اصول فقه شیعه برای رفع تعارض احادیث است.
summaries.fr: Al-Mualla ibn Khunays, proche compagnon de l'Imam al-Sadiq, lui demanda comment résoudre les narrations contradictoires des premiers et derniers Imams. L'Imam établit un principe herméneutique fondamental : la narration de l'Imam vivant (actuel) a la priorité ; et dans une autre version : prenez la narration la plus récente. Ce hadith est fondamental dans l'usul al-fiqh chiite pour résoudre les contradictions dans le corpus hadithique.
summaries.id: Al-Mualla bin Khunays, seorang sahabat dekat Imam al-Sadiq, bertanya kepada beliau tentang cara menyelesaikan perbedaan antara hadith dari Imam-Imam terdahulu dan kemudian. Imam menetapkan prinsip hermeneutis yang mendasar: hadith dari Imam yang masih hidup (saat ini) lebih diutamakan, dan dalam riwayat lain: ambillah hadith yang terbaru. Hadith ini sangat fundamental dalam usul al-fiqh Syiah untuk menyelesaikan kontradiksi dalam korpus hadith.
summaries.ru: Аль-Муалла ибн Хунайс, близкий сподвижник Имама ас-Садика, спросил его о том, как разрешать противоречия между преданиями от ранних и поздних Имамов. Имам установил ключевой герменевтический принцип: предание от живого (нынешнего) Имама имеет приоритет; а в другой версии: берите самое последнее. Этот хадис занимает основополагающее место в шиитском усул аль-фикх для разрешения противоречий в хадисном корпусе.
summaries.tr: İmam Sadık'ın yakın sahabisi el-Mualla b. Huneys, önceki ve sonraki İmamlardan gelen çelişkili rivayetlerin nasıl çözüleceğini sordu. İmam, temel bir usul prensibi ortaya koydu: Yaşayan (mevcut) İmam'ın rivayeti önceliklidir ve başka bir rivayette: En yeni hadisi alın. Bu hadis, Şia usul-ü fıkhında çelişkili hadislerin çözümünde temel öneme sahiptir.
summaries.ur: المعلی بن خنیس، امام صادق علیہ السلام کے ایک قریبی صحابی، نے ان سے پہلے اور بعد کے آئمہ کی متضاد روایات کے حل کے بارے میں پوچھا۔ امام نے ایک بنیادی اصول بیان کیا: زندہ (موجودہ) امام کی روایت مقدم ہے، اور دوسری روایت میں: سب سے جدید حدیث لو۔ یہ حدیث شیعہ اصول فقہ میں متضاد روایات کے حل کے لیے بنیادی اہمیت رکھتی ہے۔
summaries.zh: 穆阿拉·本·胡奈斯是伊玛目萨迪克的亲密伴侣，他询问如何解决来自早期和晚期伊玛目的相互矛盾的圣训。伊玛目确立了一个关键的解释原则：来自在世（当前）伊玛目的传述优先；在另一版本中：取最新的圣训。此圣训在什叶派伊斯兰法学原理中，对于解决圣训汇编中的矛盾具有根本性意义。

translation.de: euch leicht fällt/für euch möglich ist

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/221)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِذَا جَاءَ حَدِيثٌ عَنْ أَوَّلِكُمْ وَ حَدِيثٌ عَنْ آخِرِكُمْ بِأَيِّهِمَا نَأْخُذُ فَقَالَ خُذُوا بِهِ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَكُمْ عَنِ الْحَيِّ فَإِنْ بَلَغَكُمْ عَنِ الْحَيِّ فَخُذُوا بِقَوْلِهِ
قَالَ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) إِنَّا وَ اللَّهِ لَا نُدْخِلُكُمْ إِلَّا فِيمَا يَسَعُكُمْ
وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ خُذُوا بِالْأَحْدَثِ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from his father, from Ismail Bin Marrar, from Yunus, from Dawood Bin Farqad, from Al Moalla Bin Khunays who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘When there comes a Hadeeth from your<sup>asws</sup> former ones<sup>asws</sup>, and a Hadeeth from your<sup>asws</sup> later ones<sup>asws</sup>, by which of these two should we take?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said, ‘Be taking with it until there reached you all from the living one<sup>asws</sup>. So when there does reach you from the living one<sup>asws</sup>, so adhere to his<sup>asws</sup> words’.
He (the narrator) said, ‘Then Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘We<sup>asws</sup>, by Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, do not enter you all into anything except in what there is ease for you’.
And in another report, ‘(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘Take by the Hadeeth’.
en.sarwar: 9. It is narrated from him from his father from Isma’il ibn Marrar from Yunus from Dawud ibn Farqad from Mu‘alla ibn Khunays who has said the following. “I asked Imam abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.), ‘If we receive a Hadith from first of you and from the members of your later generation which one should we follow?” The Imam replied, “Follow it until you receive it from the living Imam. When it comes from a living Imam follow his words.” The Imam than said, “We by Allah, like you to follow what is easy for you.” In another Hadith it is says, “Follow whichever the newest.”’

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق تلقاه الصحاب بالقبول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (221/ 1)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلَيْنِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا بَيْنَهُمَا مُنَازَعَةٌ فِي دَيْنٍ أَوْ مِيرَاثٍ فَتَحَاكَمَا إِلَى السُّلْطَانِ وَ إِلَى الْقُضَاةِ أَ يَحِلُّ ذَلِكَ

قَالَ مَنْ تَحَاكَمَ إِلَيْهِمْ فِي حَقٍّ أَوْ بَاطِلٍ فَإِنَّمَا تَحَاكَمَ إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَ مَا يَحْكُمُ لَهُ فَإِنَّمَا يَأْخُذُ سُحْتاً وَ إِنْ كَانَ حَقّاً ثَابِتاً لِأَنَّهُ أَخَذَهُ بِحُكْمِ الطَّاغُوتِ وَ قَدْ أَمَرَ اللَّهُ أَنْ يُكْفَرَ بِهِ قَالَ اللَّهُ تَعَالَى يُرِيدُونَ أَنْ يَتَحاكَمُوا إِلَى الطَّاغُوتِ وَ قَدْ أُمِرُوا أَنْ يَكْفُرُوا بِهِ

قُلْتُ فَكَيْفَ يَصْنَعَانِ قَالَ يَنْظُرَانِ إِلَى مَنْ كَانَ مِنْكُمْ مِمَّنْ قَدْ رَوَى حَدِيثَنَا وَ نَظَرَ فِي حَلَالِنَا وَ حَرَامِنَا وَ عَرَفَ أَحْكَامَنَا فَلْيَرْضَوْا بِهِ حَكَماً فَإِنِّي قَدْ جَعَلْتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ حَاكِماً فَإِذَا حَكَمَ بِحُكْمِنَا فَلَمْ يَقْبَلْهُ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّمَا اسْتَخَفَّ بِحُكْمِ اللَّهِ وَ عَلَيْنَا رَدَّ وَ الرَّادُّ عَلَيْنَا الرَّادُّ عَلَى اللَّهِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى حَدِّ الشِّرْكِ بِاللَّهِ

قُلْتُ فَإِنْ كَانَ كُلُّ رَجُلٍ اخْتَارَ رَجُلًا مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا فَرَضِيَا أَنْ يَكُونَا النَّاظِرَيْنِ فِي حَقِّهِمَا وَ اخْتَلَفَا فِيمَا حَكَمَا وَ كِلَاهُمَا اخْتَلَفَا فِي حَدِيثِكُمْ قَالَ الْحُكْمُ مَا حَكَمَ بِهِ أَعْدَلُهُمَا وَ أَفْقَهُهُمَا وَ أَصْدَقُهُمَا فِي الْحَدِيثِ وَ أَوْرَعُهُمَا وَ لَا يَلْتَفِتْ إِلَى مَا يَحْكُمُ بِهِ الْآخَرُ

قَالَ قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُمَا عَدْلَانِ مَرْضِيَّانِ عِنْدَ أَصْحَابِنَا لَا يُفَضَّلُ وَاحِدٌ مِنْهُمَا عَلَى الْآخَرِ قَالَ فَقَالَ يُنْظَرُ إِلَى مَا كَانَ مِنْ رِوَايَتِهِمْ عَنَّا فِي ذَلِكَ الَّذِي حَكَمَا بِهِ الْمُجْمَعُ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَصْحَابِكَ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِهِ مِنْ حُكْمِنَا وَ يُتْرَكُ الشَّاذُّ الَّذِي لَيْسَ بِمَشْهُورٍ عِنْدَ أَصْحَابِكَ فَإِنَّ الْمُجْمَعَ عَلَيْهِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ

وَ إِنَّمَا الْأُمُورُ ثَلَاثَةٌ أَمْرٌ بَيِّنٌ رُشْدُهُ فَيُتَّبَعُ وَ أَمْرٌ بَيِّنٌ غَيُّهُ فَيُجْتَنَبُ وَ أَمْرٌ مُشْكِلٌ يُرَدُّ عِلْمُهُ إِلَى اللَّهِ وَ إِلَى رَسُولِهِ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) حَلَالٌ بَيِّنٌ وَ حَرَامٌ بَيِّنٌ وَ شُبُهَاتٌ بَيْنَ ذَلِكَ فَمَنْ تَرَكَ الشُّبُهَاتِ نَجَا مِنَ الْمُحَرَّمَاتِ وَ مَنْ أَخَذَ بِالشُّبُهَاتِ ارْتَكَبَ الْمُحَرَّمَاتِ وَ هَلَكَ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُ

قُلْتُ فَإِنْ كَانَ الْخَبَرَانِ عَنْكُمَا مَشْهُورَيْنِ قَدْ رَوَاهُمَا الثِّقَاتُ عَنْكُمْ قَالَ يُنْظَرُ فَمَا وَافَقَ حُكْمُهُ حُكْمَ الْكِتَابِ وَ السُّنَّةِ وَ خَالَفَ الْعَامَّةَ فَيُؤْخَذُ بِهِ وَ يُتْرَكُ مَا خَالَفَ حُكْمُهُ حُكْمَ الْكِتَابِ وَ السُّنَّةِ وَ وَافَقَ الْعَامَّةَ

قُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ الْفَقِيهَانِ عَرَفَا حُكْمَهُ مِنَ الْكِتَابِ وَ السُّنَّةِ وَ وَجَدْنَا أَحَدَ الْخَبَرَيْنِ مُوَافِقاً لِلْعَامَّةِ وَ الْآخَرَ مُخَالِفاً لَهُمْ بِأَيِّ الْخَبَرَيْنِ يُؤْخَذُ قَالَ مَا خَالَفَ الْعَامَّةَ فَفِيهِ الرَّشَادُ
فَقُلْتُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَإِنْ وَافَقَهُمَا الْخَبَرَانِ جَمِيعاً قَالَ يُنْظَرُ إِلَى مَا هُمْ إِلَيْهِ أَمْيَلُ حُكَّامُهُمْ وَ قُضَاتُهُمْ فَيُتْرَكُ وَ يُؤْخَذُ بِالْآخَرِ

قُلْتُ فَإِنْ وَافَقَ حُكَّامُهُمُ الْخَبَرَيْنِ جَمِيعاً قَالَ إِذَا كَانَ ذَلِكَ فَأَرْجِهْ حَتَّى تَلْقَى إِمَامَكَ فَإِنَّ الْوُقُوفَ عِنْدَ الشُّبُهَاتِ خَيْرٌ مِنَ الِاقْتِحَامِ فِي الْهَلَكَاتِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Isa, from Safwan Bin Yahya, from Dawood Bin Al Husayn, from Umar Bin Hanzala who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about two men from our companions between who there was a dispute regarding a debt or an inheritance. So they both adjudicated it to the ruling authority and to the judges. Is that permissible?’

Heasws said: ‘The one who adjudicates to them regarding a right or a wrong, so rather he has adjudicated to the tyrant, and whatever they adjudicate to him, so rather he has taken an ill-gotten gain, and even though it may be a proven right, because he has taken it by a judgment of the tyrant, and Allahazwj has Commanded that he disbelieves in it. [4:60] They desire to summon one another to the judgement of the tyrant, though they were Commanded to deny him.

I said, ‘So how should they both be dealing with it?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘They should both take it for consideration to the one who was from you, from the one who have reported our<sup>asws</sup> Ahadeeth and looks into our<sup>asws</sup> Pemissible and our<sup>asws</sup> Prohibition, and he recognises our<sup>asws</sup> regulations. So let them both be pleased with him as a judge, for I<sup>asws</sup> have made him to be a judge upon you (to settle their dispute). So when he does judge by our<sup>asws</sup> judgment and it is not accepted from him, so rather you would have taken lightly with the Judgment of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and upon us<sup>asws</sup> is the rejection. And the rejection upon us<sup>asws</sup>, is the rejection upon Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and he would be upon the boundary of the Shirk (association) with Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’.

I said, ‘Supposing it was so that each man chooses a man each from our companions, so they are both pleased for them both to look into their rights, and they both differ in their respective judgments, and both of them differ regarding your<sup>asws</sup> Hadeeth?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘The judgment would what is judged by the one who is more just of the two, and more understanding of the two, or the more truthful of the two regarding the Hadeeth, or the more pious of the two, and he would not turn to what the other one judged with’.

He (the narrator) said, ‘I said, ‘Supposing both of them are just, agreeable in the presence of our companions, not one of the two having any merit upon the other?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You would look at what was from their reports from us<sup>asws</sup> regarding that (issue) which they are judging with, and consensus upon it from your companions. So they should be taking with our<sup>asws</sup> judgment and neglect the abnormal which is not well-known among your companions. So the consensus would be upon which there is no doubt in it.

And rather, the matters are three (types) – a matter which is clearly righteous guidance, so you would follow it, and a matter which is clearly wrong, so you would keep aside from it, and a matter which is difficult, you would return its knowledge to Allah<sup>azwj</sup> and His<sup>azwj</sup> Rasool<sup>saww</sup>. Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The Permissible are clear, and the Prohibitions are clear, and the doubtful ones are between that. So the one who neglects the doubtful would be saved from the Prohibitions, and the one who takes the doubtful would indulge in the Prohibitions and be destroyed from where he does not even know’.

I said, ‘Supposing there are two Ahadeeth from you, both well known, both having been reported by the trustworthy ones from you<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It would be looked into. So whatever judgment was in accordance with the Judgment of the Book and the Sunnah, and differs from the general Muslims, so it would be taken with, and whatever judgment was different from the Judgment of the Book and the Sunnah, and in accordance with the general Muslims, would be neglected’.

I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! What is your<sup>asws</sup> view if they were both understanding ones, both recognising its judgment from the Book and the Sunnah, and we find one of the two Ahadeeth in accordance with the general Muslims and the other one opposed to them. With which of the two Ahadeeth would be it taken?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whatever opposes the general Muslims, for there would be righteous guidance therein’.

So I said, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! Supposing if both of the two Ahadeeth are in accordance together?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It would be looked at what their rulers and their judges are inclined towards, so you would leave it, and be taking with the other one’.

I said, ‘Supposing their rulers and their judges are all concordant?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When it was that, so wait until you meet your Imam<sup>asws</sup>, for the pausing during the doubtful matters is better than storming into the destruction’.
en.sarwar: 10. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Safwan ibn Yahya from Dawud ibn al-Husayn from ‘Umar ibn Hanzala who has said the following. “I asked Imam abu ‘Abdallah (a.s.) about the two people with a dispute between them on the issue of debts or inheritance and they go to the king or the judges for a decision is it permissible to seek such decisions?” The Imam replied, “Whoever would go to them for a judgement in a right or wrongful matter it is like seeking the judgment of the devil. Anything received through such judgment would like consuming filth even if it would one’s established right. It is because of receiving through the judgment of the devil and Allah has commanded to reject the devil, “yet choose to take their affairs to Satan for judgment even though they are commanded to deny him. Satan wants to lead them far away from the right path. [4:60]” I said, “What should then they do?” The Imam replied, “They must look for one among you who have narrated our Hadith and have studied what is lawful and unlawful in our teachings and have learned our laws they must agree to settle their dispute according to his judgment because I have made him over you a ruler. When he may judge according to our commands and then it is not accepted from him the dissenting this judgment has ignored the commands of Allah and it is rejection of us. Rejecting us is rejecting Allah and that is up to the level of paganism and considering things equal to Allah.” I said, “What if each one of such disputing parties would chose a man from among our people and agree to accept their judgment but these two man would come up with different judgments and they would have differences in your Hadith?” The Imam replied, “The judgment will be the judgment of the one who has a more just, having more better understanding of the law, Fiqh, the more truthful in Hadith and the more pious of the two. The judgment of the other one will be disregarded.” I said, “What if both (of such judges) would be just and accepted among our people and none of them would have been any preference over the other?” The Imam replied, “One must consider and study the hadith that each one of them would narrate from us as to which has received the acceptance of all of your people. Such Hadith must be followed and the one, which rarely accepted and is not popular in your people, must be disregarded because the one popularly accepted is free of doubts. The nature of cases are of three kinds: (a) A case that is a well-known and true to follow. (b) A case that is well known to be false to stay away from. (c) And a confusing case the knowledge of which must be left to Allah and His Prophet for an answer. The holy Prophet has said, ‘There is the clearly lawful and the clearly unlawful and the confusing cases. One who stays away from the confusing ones he has protected himself against the unlawful ones. Those who follow the confusing matters they indulge in unlawful matters and will be destroyed unexpectedly.” I said, “What if both Hadith from you would be popular and narrated by the trustworthy people from you?” The Imam replied, “One must study to find out which one agrees with the laws of the Quran and the Sunnah and it does not agree with the laws of the those who oppose us. Such Hadith must be accepted and the one that disagree with the laws of the Quran and the Sunnah and coincides the masses must be disregarded.” I said, “May Allah take my soul in the service of your cause, What if both Faqih, scholars of the law would have deduced and learned their judgment from the book and the Sunnah and found that one of the Hadith agrees with the masses and the other disagrees with the masses which one must be followed?” The Imam replied, “The one which disagrees with the masses must be followed because in it there is guidance.” I said, “May Allah take my soul in the service of your cause, what if both Hadith would agree with the masses?” The Imam replied, “One must study to find out of the two the one that is more agreeable to their rulers and judges must be disregarded and the other must be followed.” I said, “What if both Hadith would agree with their rulers?” The Imam replied, “If such would be the case it must be suspended until you meet your Imam. Restraint in confusing cases is better than indulging in destruction.”
